ref: In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.
text: Le gɔmedzedzea me Mawu wɔ dziƒo kple anyigba.

ref: The earth was barren, with no form of life; it was under a roaring ocean covered with darkness. But the Spirit of God was moving over the water. The First Day
text: Ke anyigba le nyama, nɔnɔme meli nɛ o, ele ƒuƒlu, eye viviti do ɖe tsi gbana la ŋku me, eye Mawu ƒe Gbɔgbɔ le agba sam le tsia dzi.

ref: God said, “I command light to shine!” And light started shining.
text: Eye Mawu gblɔ be: Kekeli nedo! Eye kekeli do.

ref: God looked at the light and saw that it was good. He separated light from darkness
text: Eye Mawu kpɔ kekelia bena enyo. Eye Mawu ma kekeli la kple viviti la dome.

ref: and named the light “Day” and the darkness “Night.” Evening came, then morning—that was the first day. The Second Day
text: Mawu yɔ kekeli la be ŋkeke; ke viviti la eyɔe be zã. Ale zã do, eye ŋu ke, eya nye ŋkeke gbãtɔ.

ref: God said, “I command a dome to separate the water above it from the water below it.”
text: Eye Mawu gblɔ be: Alili nenɔ tsia titina, be wòanye dometsonu anɔ tsi kple tsi dome!

ref: and named it “Sky.” Evening came, then morning—that was the second day. The Third Day
text: Eye Mawu yɔ alilia be dziƒo. Ale zã do, eye ŋu ke, eya nye ŋkeke evelia.

ref: God said, “I command the water under the sky to come together in one place, so there will be dry ground.” And that's what happened.
text: Eye Mawu gblɔ be: Tsi, si le dziƒoa te la, neƒo ƒu ɖe teƒe ɖeka, bena ƒuƒuiƒe nadze! Eye wòva me nenema.

ref: God named the dry ground “Land,” and he named the water “Ocean.” God looked at what he had done and saw that it was good.
text: Mawu yɔ ƒuƒuiƒe la be anyigba, ke tsi ƒoƒua la eyɔe be atsiaƒu. Eye Mawu kpo bena enyo.

ref: God said, “I command the earth to produce all kinds of plants, including fruit trees and grain.” And that's what happened.
text: Eye Mawu gblɔ be: Anyigba nemie gbe fẽwo, nu miemie, siwo tsea ku, kple ati tseku, siwo tsena wo tɔ wo tɔwoe, esiwo me ku le, le anyigba la dzi. Eye wòva me nenema.

ref: The earth produced all kinds of vegetation. God looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: Anyigba mie gbe fẽwo, nu miemie, siwo tsea ku wo tɔ wo tɔwoe, kple ati, siwo tsena, siwo me ku le, wo tɔ wo tɔwoe. Eye Mawu kpo bena enyo.

ref: Evening came, then morning—that was the third day. The Fourth Day
text: Ale zã do, eye ŋu ke, eya nye ŋkeke etɔ̃lia.

ref: God said, “I command lights to appear in the sky and to separate day from night and to show the time for seasons, special days, and years.
text: Eye Mawu gblɔ be: Nu keklẽwo nado ɖe dziƒolilia ŋu, bena woade vovo ŋkeke kple zã dome, eye woanɔ anyi anye dzesiwo, afia azãgbewo, ŋkekewo kple ƒewo,

ref: I command them to shine on the earth.” And that's what happened.
text: eye woanye nu keklẽwo anɔ dziƒolilia ŋu, bene woaklẽ ɖe anyigba dzi! Eye wòva me nenem.

ref: God made two powerful lights, the brighter one to rule the day and the other to rule the night. He also made the stars.
text: Tete Mawu wɔ nu keklẽ gã eveawo. Ewɔ gãtɔ, bena woaɖu ŋkeke dzi, kple suetɔ, bena woaɖu zã dzi, hekpe ɖe ɣletiviwo ŋu.

ref: Then God put these lights in the sky to shine on the earth,
text: Eye Mawu tsɔ wo ɖo dziƒolilia ŋu, bena woaklẽ ɖe anyigba dzi,

ref: to rule day and night, and to separate light from darkness. God looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: woaɖu ŋkeke kple zã dzi, eye woamã kekeli kple viviti dome. Eye Mawu kpɔ bena enyo.

ref: Evening came, then morning—that was the fourth day. The Fifth Day
text: Ale zã do, eye ŋu ke, eya nye ŋkeke enelia.

ref: God said, “I command the ocean to be full of living creatures, and I command birds to fly above the earth.”
text: Tete Mawu gblɔ bena: Nu gbagbewo neƒu tsi le tsia me, eye xe dzodzoewo nedzo le ya me le dziƒolilia ƒe ŋku me.

ref: So God made the giant sea monsters and all the living creatures that swim in the ocean. He also made every kind of bird. God looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: Eye Mawu wo ƒumelã klitsuwo kple nu gbagbe, siwo katã tana, eye woʋãna le tsia me, hamehame, kple xe, siwo katã dzona. Eye Mawu kpo bena enyo.

ref: Then he gave the living creatures his blessing—he told the ocean creatures to increase and live everywhere in the ocean and the birds to increase everywhere on earth.
text: Eye Mawu yra wo gblɔ bena: Midzi, ne miasɔ gbɔ, eye miayɔ atsiaƒutsia me, eye xe dzodzoeawo nasɔ gbã le anyigba dzi!

ref: Evening came, then morning—that was the fifth day. The Sixth Day
text: Ale zã do, eye ŋu ke, eya nye ŋkeke atɔ̃lia.

ref: God said, “I command the earth to give life to all kinds of tame animals, wild animals, and reptiles.” And that's what happened.
text: Eye Mawu gblɔ bena: Nu gbagbewo nedo tso anyigba me hamehame, aƒemelãwo kple nu tatawo kple gbemelãwo hamehame!

ref: God made every one of them. Then he looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: Ale Mawu wɔ gbemelãwo hamehame, aƒemelãwo hamehame kple lã, siwo katã tana le anyigba dzi hamehame. Eye Mawu kpɔ bena enyo. Mawu wɔ ame le eya ŋutɔ ƒe nɔnɔme nu.

ref: God said, “Now we will make humans, and they will be like us. We will let them rule the fish, the birds, and all other living creatures.”
text: Eye Mawu gblɔ be: Mine míawɔ ame ɖe míaƒe nɔnɔme, woaɖi mí, eye wòaɖu ƒumelãwo, dziƒoxe dzodzoewo, aƒemelãwo kple gbemelãwo katã kpakple lã, siwo katã tana le anyigba dzi la dzi!

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Eye Mawu wɔ ame ɖe ye ŋutɔ ƒe nɔnɔme, Mawu ƒe nɔnɔme nue wòwɔe ɖo; ŋutsu kple nyɔnu wòwɔ.

ref: God gave them his blessing and said: Have a lot of children! Fill the earth with people and bring it under your control. Rule over the fish in the ocean, the birds in the sky, and every animal on the earth.
text: Eye Mawu yra wo, eye Mawu gblɔ na wo bena: Midzi, ne miasɔ gbɔ, eye miayɔ anyigba la dzi, eye mianye agbo ɖe edzi, miaɖu ƒumelãwo kple dziƒoxe dzodzoewo kple lã, siwo katã zɔna le anyigba dzi la dzi!

ref: And I have given the green plants as food for everything else that breathes, including animals, both wild and tame, and birds. And so it was.
text: ke gbewo kple amagbewo katã nanye nuɖuɖu na anyigba dzi lãwo katã kple dziƒoxe dzodzoewo katã kple nusiwo katã tana, siwo me agbe le, le anyigba dzi. Eye wòva me nenema.

ref: God looked at what he had done. All of it was very good! Evening came, then morning—that was the sixth day.
text: Eye Mawu kpo nusiwo katã wòwɔ la, eye kpɔ ɖa, wonye ŋutɔ. Ale zã do, eye ŋu ke, eya nye ŋkeke adelia.

ref: So the heavens and the earth and everything else were created. The Seventh Day
text: Ale wowu dziƒo kple anyigba kple wo me nu gbogboawo katã nu enye si.

ref: By the seventh day God had finished his work, and so he rested.
text: Eye Mawu wu eƒe ɖo, si wòwɔ la nu le ŋkeke adreagbe, eye wòdzudzɔ le ŋkeke adreagbe tso eƒe do, siwo katã wòwɔ la me.

ref: God blessed the seventh day and made it special, because on that day he rested from his work.
text: Eye Mawu yra ŋkeke adrelia, eye wòkɔ eŋuti, elabena eya dzi wòdzudzɔ eɖokui le tso eƒe dɔwɔwɔ, siwo katã Mawu wɔ, eye wòna wodo la me. Amegbetɔ le Paradiso.

ref: That's how God created the heavens and the earth. The Garden of Eden When the Lord God made the heavens and the earth,
text: Esia nye dziƒo kple anyigba ƒe dzɔdzɔmenyawo le wo wɔwɔ me le ɣeyiɣi, si me Yehowa Mawu wo anyigba kple dziƒo.

ref: no grass or plants were growing anywhere. God had not yet sent any rain, and there was no one to work the land.
text: Ke gbemetia ɖeke mele anyigba dzi haɖe o, eye nu miemiea ɖeke medo ɖe gbe me haɖe o; elabena Yehowa Mawu mena tsi dza ɖe anyigba dzi haɖe o, eye ame meli ade anyigba la o.

ref: But streams came up from the ground and watered the earth.
text: Eye afu do tso anyigba dzi, eye wònyɔ ɖe anyigba la katã ƒe ŋku me.

ref: The Lord God took some soil from the ground and made a man. God breathed life into the man, and the man started breathing.
text: Azã Yehowa Mawu tsɔ anyi mè ame, eye wògbɔ agbegbɔgbɔ ɖe eƒe ŋɔti me; ale ame la zu luʋɔ gbagbe.

ref: The Lord made a garden in a place called Eden, which was in the east, and he put the man there.
text: Tete Yehowa Mawu tso abɔ ɖe Eden le ɣedzeƒe, eye wotsɔ ame, si wòmè la, ɖo afima.

ref: The Lord God filled the garden with all kinds of beautiful trees and fruit trees. Two other trees were in the middle of the garden. One of these gave life—the other gave the wisdom to know the difference between right and wrong.
text: Eye Yehowa Mawu na ati vovovo, siwo nya kpɔna, siwo ƒe tsetse nyo na ɖuɖu la, mie tso anyigba me; eye agbeti la kpakple sidzedze nyui kple vɔ̃ ƒe ati la le abɔ la titina.

ref: From Eden a river flowed out to water the garden, then it divided into four rivers.
text: Eye tɔsisia ɖe do tso Eden, be woade tsi abɔ la; eye tso afima wòdze ɖe alɔ ene me.

ref: The first one is the Pishon River that flows through the land of Havilah,
text: Gbãtɔ ŋkɔ nye Pison; eƒo xlã Xawila-nyigba blibo la katã; afima sika le;

ref: where pure gold, rare perfumes, and precious stones are found.
text: eye anyigba sia ƒe sika nyo ŋutɔ; afima bedolax- kple soham-kpe hã le.

ref: The second is the Gihon River that winds through Ethiopia.
text: Tɔsisi evelia ŋkɔ nye Gixon; eƒo xlã Kus-nyigba blibo la katã.

ref: The Tigris River that flows east of Assyria is the third, and the fourth is the Euphrates River.
text: Tɔsisi etɔ̃lia ŋkɔ nye Xidekel; esi to Asur ƒe ɣedzeƒe gome. Eye tɔsisi enelia nye Frat.

ref: The Lord God put the man in the Garden of Eden to take care of it and to look after it.
text: Eye Yehowa Mawu tsɔ ame la ɖo Eɖen-bɔ me, be woaŋlɔ eme, eye wòakpɔ edzi. Mawu fe sedede.

ref: But the Lord told him, “You may eat fruit from any tree in the garden,
text: Eye Yehowa Mawu de se na ame gblɔ bena: Ɖu abɔmetiawo katã ƒe tsetse faa;

ref: except the one that has the power to let you know the difference between right and wrong. If you eat any fruit from that tree, you will die before the day is over!”
text: ke ati, si na wodzea si nyui kple vɔ la, megaɖu eya ƒe tsetsea ɖeke o; gbesigbe naɖu eƒe ɖe, ku ke naku.

ref: The Lord God said, “It isn't good for the man to live alone. I will make a suitable partner for him.”
text: Eye Yehowa Mawu gblɔ bena: Menyo bena ame la natsi akogo o; mawɔ kpeɖeŋutɔ, si asɔ ɖe eŋu la nɛ.

ref: So the Lord God made him fall into a deep sleep, and he took out one of the man's ribs. Then after closing the man's side,
text: Eye Yehowa Mawu na ame dɔ alɔ̃ yi me ʋĩ, eye wòɖe eƒe agbaƒutiwo dometɔ ɖeka, eye wotsɔ lã ɖo eteƒe.

ref: the Lord made a woman out of the rib. The Lord God brought her to the man,
text: Tete Yehowa Mawu tsɔ agbaƒuti, si wòɖe le amea me la, wɔ nyɔnui, eye wòkplɔe va ame la gbɔe.

ref: and the man exclaimed, “Here is someone like me! She is part of my body, my own flesh and bones. She came from me, a man. So I will name her Woman!”
text: Eye ame la gblɔ be: Azɔ ko! Esia enye ƒu tso nye ƒu me kple lã tso nye lã me; woayɔ ame sia bena nyɔnu, elabena ŋutsu mee woɖee tsoe.

ref: That's why a man will leave his own father and mother. He marries a woman, and the two of them become like one person.
text: Eya ŋuti ŋutsu agblẽ fofoa kple dadaa ɖi ahaku ɖe srɔ̃a ŋuti, eye woanye ŋutilã ɖeka.

ref: Although the man and his wife were both naked, they were not ashamed.
text: Eye wo kpakple eve la wole ƒeƒle, ame la kple srɔ̃a, eye ŋu mekpea wo o.

ref: The snake was sneakier than any of the other wild animals that the Lord God had made. One day it came to the woman and asked, “Did God tell you not to eat fruit from any tree in the garden?”
text: Ke da dze aye wu gbemelã, siwo katã Yehowa Mawu wɔ la, eye wògblɔ na nyɔnu la bena: Vavãe Mawu gblɔ bena, migaɖu abɔmetiawo katã ƒe tsetsea ɖeke o mahã?

ref: The woman answered, “God said we could eat fruit from any tree in the garden,
text: Nyɔnua ɖo ŋu na da la be: Míeɖua abɔmetiawo ƒe tsetse faa;

ref: except the one in the middle. He told us not to eat fruit from that tree or even to touch it. If we do, we will die.”
text: ke Mawu gblɔ le ati, si le abɔa titina la ƒe tsetse ŋuti bena: Migaɖu eƒe ɖeke o, eye migaka asi eŋu hã o, be miagaku o!

ref: “No, you won't!” the snake replied.
text: Eye da la gblɔ na nyɔnua bena: Miele kuku ge akpɔ o;

ref: “God understands what will happen on the day you eat fruit from that tree. You will see what you have done, and you will know the difference between right and wrong, just as God does.”
text: ke boŋ Mawu nyae bena, gbesigbe ke miaɖu eƒe ɖe la, miaƒe ŋkuwo laʋu, eye mianɔ abe Mawu ene, anya nyui kple vɔ̃.

ref: The woman stared at the fruit. It looked beautiful and tasty. She wanted the wisdom that it would give her, and she ate some of the fruit. Her husband was there with her, so she gave some to him, and he ate it too.
text: Esi nyɔnu la kpɔ bena, ati la nyo na ɖuɖu, ebiã ŋu na ame, eye wòdzroa ame, eye wònana wodzea nunya la, egbe eƒe tsetsea ɖe heɖu, eye wòna srɔ̃a, si le egbɔ hã woɖu.

ref: At once they saw what they had done, and they realized they were naked. Then they sewed fig leaves together to cover themselves.
text: Tete wo kpakple eve ƒe ŋkuwo ʋu, eye wodze sii bena, yewole amama, eye wòtɔ amagbawo hesa ɖe ali.

ref: Late in the afternoon, when the breeze began to blow, the man and woman heard the Lord God walking in the garden. So they hid behind some trees. Sin Brings a Curse
text: Eye esi wose Yehowa Mawu ŋkɔ le anyinufaɣi le abɔa me la, amea kple srɔ̃a woɣla wo ɖokui ɖe Yehowa Mawu ɖe abɔmetia ɖe te.

ref: The Lord God called out to the man and asked, “Where are you?”
text: Tete Yehowa Mawu yɔ amea gblɔ nɛ bena: Afika nèle?

ref: The man answered, “I was naked, and when I heard you walking through the garden, I was frightened and hid!”
text: Eɖo ŋu nɛ bena: Mese ŋkɔwò le abɔa me, eye meva; elabena mele amama, eye meɣla ɖokuinye.

ref: “How did you know you were naked?” God asked. “Did you eat any fruit from that tree in the middle of the garden?”
text: Egblɔ be: Ameka toe na wò be, amamae nèle? Alo ɖe nèɖu ati, si ŋuti meɖe se na wò be, megaɖu eƒe ɖeke o la ƒe ɖe mahã?

ref: “It was the woman you put here with me,” the man said. “She gave me some of the fruit, and I ate it.”
text: Ame la ɖo ŋu bena: Nyɔnu, si nètsɔ nam la, eyae na atia ɖem meɖu.

ref: The Lord God then asked the woman, “What have you done?” “The snake tricked me,” she answered, “and I ate some of that fruit.”
text: Eye Yehowa Mawu gblɔ na nyɔnua bena: Nukaŋuti nèwɔ alea? Nyɔnu la ɖo ŋu ne be: Da lae blem, eye meɖui. Fiƒode kple ŋugbedodo gbãtɔ.

ref: So the Lord God said to the snake: “Because of what you have done, you will be the only animal to suffer this curse— For as long as you live, you will crawl on your stomach and eat dirt.
text: Eye Yehowa Mawu gblɔ na da la bena: Esi nèwɔ alea la, eyata woaƒo fi ade wò le aƒemelãwo kple gbemelãwo katã dome: Wò ƒodo dzi nazɔ, eye ʋuʋudedie naɖu le wò agbe me ŋkekewo katã me!

ref: You and this woman will hate each other; your descendants and hers will always be enemies. One of hers will strike you on the head, and you will strike him on the heel.”
text: Made adikã mia kple nyɔnu la dome, wò dzidzime kple nyɔnu la ƒe dzidzime dome, eya la agbã ta na wò, eye wò la àɖu afɔkpodzi nɛ.

ref: Then the Lord God said to the woman, “You will suffer terribly when you give birth. But you will still desire your husband, and he will rule over you.”
text: Eye wògblɔ na nyɔnua bena: Mana nakpe fu geɖe le fufɔfɔ me, alé ku hafi àdzi vi, ke wò tame anɔ srɔ̃wò ŋu, eye eya anye gã ɖe dziwò.

ref: The Lord said to the man, “You listened to your wife and ate the fruit I told you not to eat. And so, the ground will be under a curse because of what you did. As long as you live, you will have to struggle to grow enough food.
text: Eye wògblɔ na Adam bena: Esi nèse srɔ̃wo ƒe gbe, eye nèɖu ati, si ŋuti mede se na wò be, megaɖu eƒe ɖekeɖeke o la ƒe ɖe la, eyata woaƒo fi ade anyigba le tawò, àkpe fu hafi akpɔ nu àɖu tso me le wò agbe me ŋkekewo katã me.

ref: Your food will be plants, but the ground will produce thorns and thistles.
text: Ŋu kple aŋɔka amie na wò, eye naɖu anyigba dzi amagbewo!

ref: You will sweat all your life to earn a living; you were made out of soil, and you will once again turn into soil.”
text: Fifia ate wò, hafi nakpɔ abolo aɖu, vaseɖe esime nagatrɔ azu anyi, elabena eya mee woɖe wò tsoe. Anyi nènye, eye anyi ke nagatrɔ azu!

ref: The man Adam named his wife Eve because she would become the mother of all who live.
text: Eye ame la tsɔ ŋkɔ na srɔ̃a bena Xawa, elabena eya ava nye ame gbagbewo katã dada.

ref: Then the Lord God made clothes out of animal skins for the man and his wife.
text: Eye Yehowa Mawu tsɔ lãgbalẽwu do na Adam kple srɔ̃a.

ref: The Lord said, “They now know the difference between right and wrong, just as we do. But they must not be allowed to eat fruit from the tree that lets them live forever.”
text: Eye Yehowa Mawu gblɔ be: Kpɔ ɖa, ame zu sigbe mía dometɔ ɖeka ene, eye wònya nyui kple vɔ. Ke be wòagaɖo asi ɖa agbe agbetia hã ƒe ɖe aɖu, eye wòanɔ agbe ɖoɖoɖo o la,

ref: So the Lord God sent them out of the Garden of Eden, where they would have to work the ground from which the man had been made.
text: Yehowa Mawu ɖe ame ɖa tso Eden-bɔ la me, bene woaŋlɔ anyigba, si me woɖee tsoe.

ref: Then God put winged creatures at the entrance to the garden and a flaming, flashing sword to guard the way to the life-giving tree.
text: Tete wònyã ame la, eye wòna Kerubiwo tsɔ yi, si trɔa eɖokui, eye wòklẽna abe dzo ƒe aɖe ene la, ɖanɔ Eden-bɔ la ƒe ɣedzeƒe gome, bena woadzɔ agbeti la mɔ nu.

ref: Adam and Eve had a son. Then Eve said, “I'll name him Cain because I got him with the help of the Lord.”
text: Eye Adam ɖo kple srɔ̃a Xawa, eye wòfɔ fu dzi Kain, eye wògblɔ bena: Yehowa ɖi nam be, medzi ŋutsu.

ref: Later she had another son and named him Abel. Abel became a sheep farmer, but Cain farmed the land.
text: Emegbe la edzi enɔvia Habel, eye Habel va zu lãnyila, ke Kain ya zu agbledela.

ref: One day, Cain gave part of his harvest to the Lord,
text: Esia megbe la eteƒe medidi o, Kain tsɔ agblemenua ɖewo wɔ vɔsae na Yehowa.

ref: and Abel also gave an offering to the Lord. He killed the first-born lamb from one of his sheep and gave the Lord the best parts of it. The Lord was pleased with Abel and his offering,
text: Habel hã tsɔ eƒe lãwo ƒe ŋgɔgbevi, siwo dami la ƒe ɖewo vɛ. Eye Habel kple eƒe nunana dze Yehowa ŋu;

ref: but not with Cain and his offering. This made Cain so angry that he could not hide his feelings.
text: ke Kain kple eƒe nunana ya medze eŋu o. Eye Kain bi dzi ŋutɔ, eye wòɖo adã.

ref: The Lord said to Cain: What's wrong with you? Why do you look so angry?
text: Tete Yehowa gblɔ na Kain bena: Nukaŋuti nèle dzi bim, eye nukaŋuti nèle adã ɖom?

ref: If you had done the right thing, you would be smiling. But you did the wrong thing, and now sin is waiting to attack you like a lion. Sin wants to destroy you, but don't let it!
text: Alo menye ale wòle oa? Ne èwɔ nyui la, wò ŋku me akɔ, ke ne mèwɔ nyui o la, ekema nuvɔ̃ de xa ɖe ʋɔtru nu, eye wòɖo tamea ɖe ɖe ŋuwò; ke wò la ɖu edzi! Kain wu nɔvia.

ref: Cain said to his brother Abel, “Let's go for a walk.” And when they were out in a field, Cain attacked and killed him.
text: Esia megbe la Kain gadɔ nu kple nɔvia Habel. Ke eva ve me bena, esime wole gbe me la, Kain tso ɖe nɔvia Habel ŋuti, eye wòwui.

ref: Afterwards the Lord asked Cain, “Where is Abel?” “How should I know?” he answered. “Am I supposed to look after my brother?”
text: Tete Yehowa gblɔ na Kain bena: Afika noviwò Habel le? Eye wòɖo ŋu nɛ be: Nyemenyae o! Alo nɔvinye dzikpɔla menyea?

ref: Then the Lord said: Why have you done this terrible thing? You killed your own brother, and his blood flowed onto the ground. Now his blood is calling out for me to punish you.
text: Eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Nuka enye esi nèwɔ? Kpɔ ɖa, nɔviwò ƒe ʋu, si nèkɔ ɖe anyi la ƒe gbe le ɖiɖim tso anyigba dzi mese!

ref: And so, I'll put you under a curse. Because you killed Abel and made his blood run out on the ground, you will never be able to farm it again.
text: Azã la woaƒo fi ade wò, adzo le anyigba, si ke eƒe nu xɔ nɔviwò ƒe ʋu le asiwò no la dzi.

ref: When you try to farm the land, it won't produce anything for you. From now on, you'll be without a home, and you'll spend the rest of your life wandering from place to place.
text: Ne eŋlɔ anyigba la, megale nu ʋẽ ge na wò o; tsatsala kple godzela nanye le anyigba dzi!

ref: “This punishment is too hard!” Cain said.
text: Eye Kain gblɔ na Yehowa bena: Nye vodada la kpe wu nye tsɔtsɔ.

ref: “You're making me leave my home and live far from you. I will have to wander about without a home, and just anyone could kill me.”
text: Wòe nyãm egbe tso agbledenyigba la dzi; madzo le wò ŋkuta anye tsatsala kple godzela le anyigba dzi, eye ava me bena, amesiame, si akpɔm la, awum.

ref: “No!” the Lord answered. “Anyone who kills you will be punished seven times worse than I am punishing you.” So the Lord put a mark on Cain to warn everyone not to kill him.
text: Eye Yehowa gblɔ nɛ bena: Eyata amesiame, si awu Kain la, woabia hlɔ̃e zi gbɔ zi adre. Eye Yehowa de dzesi Kain, bena amesiame, si ado goe la, nagawui o.

ref: But Cain had to go far from the Lord and live in the Land of Wandering, which is east of Eden. More and More People
text: Eye Kain dzo le Yehowa ƒe ŋku me, eye wòva nɔ Nod-nyigba dzi le Eden ƒe ɣedzeƒe gome. Kain ƒe dzidzimeviwo

ref: Later, Cain and his wife had a son named Enoch. At the time Cain was building a town, and so he named it Enoch after his son.
text: Eye Kain da kple srɔ̃a, eye wòfɔ fu dzi Henox. Eye wòtso du, tso via ƒe ŋkɔ na du la bena Henox.

ref: Then Enoch had a son named Irad, who had a son named Mehujael, who had a son named Methushael, who had a son named Lamech.
text: Eye wodzi Irad na Henox, eye Irad dzi Mehuyael, eye Mehuyael dzi Metusael, eye Metusael dzi Lamex.

ref: Lamech and Zillah had a son named Tubal Cain who made tools out of bronze and iron. They also had a daughter, whose name was Naamah.
text: Eye Zila hã dzi Tubalkain, amesi nye akɔbli- kple gaŋudowolawo fofo; eye Tubalkain nɔvinyɔnu ŋkɔ nye Naama.

ref: One day, Lamech said to his two wives, “A young man wounded me, and I killed him.
text: Eye Lamex gblɔ na srɔ̃wo bena: Ada kple Zila, miɖo to nye gbe; mi Lamex srɔ̃wo, mise ye nyawo! Vavã, mewu ŋutsu ɖe nye abi teƒe kple ɖekakpui ɖe nye dodro teƒe.

ref: Anyone who tries to get even with me will be punished ten times more than anyone who tries to get even with Cain.”
text: Elabena wòbia hlɔ̃ Kain zi adre, ke Lamex la zi blaadre vɔ adre. Set kple Enos.

ref: Adam and his wife had another son. They named him Seth, because they said, “God has given us a son to take the place of Abel, who was killed by his brother Cain.”
text: Eye Adam gadɔ kple srɔ̃a, eye wòdzi ŋutsuvi, eye wòna ŋkɔe bena Set; elabena egblɔ be: Mawu ɖo dzidzime bubu Habel, amesi Kain wu la teƒe nam.

ref: Later, Seth had a son and named him Enosh. About this time people started worshiping the Lord.
text: Eye wodzi ŋutsuvi na Set hã, eye wòna ŋkɔe bena Enos. Le ɣemaɣi me la wode asi Yehowa ŋkɔ yɔyɔ me.

ref: God created men and women to be like himself. He gave them his blessing and called them human beings. The following is a list of the descendants of Adam, the first man:
text: Ewɔ wo wonye ŋutsu kple nyɔnu, eye wòyra wo: eye wòna ŋkɔ wo bena „ame“ le gbesigbe wòwɔ wo.

ref: and God took him away at the age of
text: Esi Henox zɔ kple Mawu ta la, ebu vĩ, elabena Mawu kplɔe dzoe.

ref: Lamech said, “I'll name him Noah because he will give us comfort, as we struggle hard to make a living on this land that the Lord has put under a curse.”
text: Eye wòna ŋkɔe bena Noa, elabena egblɔ be: Ame sia ana gbɔdzɔe mí tso míaƒe dɔwɔwɔ kple fuɖeɖewo me le anyigba, si Yehowa ƒo fi de la dzi.

ref: More and more people were born, until finally they spread all over the earth. Some of their daughters were so beautiful that supernatural beings came down and married the ones they wanted.
text: mawuviwo kpɔ bena, amegbetɔviwo ƒe nyɔnuwo dze tugbe ŋutɔ, eye woɖe wo dometɔ, siwo katã dze wo ŋu.

ref: Then the Lord said, “I won't let my life-giving breath remain in anyone forever. No one will live for more than
text: Eye Yehowa gblɔ bena: Nye gbɔgbɔ maganɔ amewo me boo o, elabena ŋutilã wonye le woƒe vodada me, eye woƒe agbe me ŋkekewo nanye ƒe alafa ɖeka kple blave!

ref: The children of the supernatural beings who had married these women became famous heroes and warriors. They were called Nephilim and lived on the earth at that time and even later.
text: Le ŋkeke mawo me la ame dzɔtsuwo le anyigba dzi; eye esi Mawu viŋutsuwo ɖe amegbetɔwo ƒe vinyɔnuwo, eye wodzi vi na wo megbe la hã wogale; woawo nye ame kako ŋkɔxɔla, siwo nɔ agbe le blema.

ref: The Lord saw how bad the people on earth were and that everything they thought and planned was evil.
text: Eye esi Yehowa kpɔ bena, amegbetɔwo ƒe vɔɖivɔ̃ɖi va sɔ gbɔ le anyigba dzi, eye woƒe tamesusuwo kple dzimedidiwo nye vɔ̃ ko ɖaa la,

ref: He was sorry that he had made them,
text: eve Yehowa bena, wòwɔ ame ɖe anyigba dzi, eye woɖu dzi nɛ.

ref: and he said, “I'm going to destroy every person on earth! I'll even wipe out animals, birds, and reptiles. I'm sorry I ever made them.”
text: Eye Yehowa gblɔ bena: Matsrɔ̃ ame, siwo mewɔ la ɖa le anyigba blibo la katã dzi, tso amewo kple lãwo dzi vaseɖe nu tatawo kple dziƒoxe dzodzoewo dzi, elabena evem bena, mewɔ wo.

ref: But the Lord was pleased with Noah,
text: Ke Yehowa ve Noa nu. Noa.

ref: and this is the story about him. Noah was the only person who lived right and obeyed God.
text: Esia enye Noa ƒe dzidzimeviwo ŋutinya Noa enye ame dzɔdzɔe kple ame maɖifɔ le eŋɔlimetɔwo dome, eye Noa zɔ kple Mawu.

ref: He had three sons: Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
text: Eye Noa dzi ŋutsuvi etɔ̃: Sem, Xam kple Yafet.

ref: Get some good lumber and build a boat. Put rooms in it and cover it with tar inside and out.
text: Tsɔ dzatiwo kpa aɖakaʋu na ɖokuiwò, eye natso eme keŋ, atsɔ aŋɔ atre eme kple ekɔgo.

ref: Noah did everything God told him to do.
text: Eye Noa wɔe; alesi Mawu de se nɛ la, nenema pɛ wòwɔ.

ref: I'm going to send a flood that will destroy everything that breathes! Nothing will be left alive.
text: Elabena nye la mana tsi naɖe anyigba la, bena ŋutilã, siwo katã le dziƒoa te, esiwo me agbegbɔgbɔ le la, natsrɔ̃, eye anyigba dzi nuwo katã naku.

ref: But I solemnly promise that you, your wife, your sons, and your daughters-in-law will be kept safe in the boat.
text: Ke mabia nu kpli wò bena, nayi ɖe aɖakaʋu la me, wò ŋutɔ, viwòŋutsuwo kple srɔ̃wò hekpe ɖe viwòŋutsuawo srɔ̃wo ŋu.

ref: Take into the boat with you a male and a female of every kind of animal and bird, as well as a male and a female of every reptile. I don't want them to be destroyed.
text: Dziƒoxe dzodzoewo le woƒe ƒomeviwo nu, aƒemelãwo le woƒe ƒomeviwo nu kple nu, siwo katã tana le anyigba la dzi, le woƒe ƒomeviwo nu, wo dome eveveve woava gbɔwò, bena woanɔ agbe.

ref: The Lord told Noah: Take your whole family with you into the boat, because you are the only one on this earth who pleases me.
text: Tete Yehowa gblɔ na Noa be na: Yi ɖe aɖakaʋu la me, wò ŋutɔ kple aƒewò me katã, elabena mekpɔ bena, ame dzɔdzɔe nènye le dzidzime sia dome.

ref: Take seven pairs of every kind of animal that can be used for sacrifice and one pair of all others.
text: Ɖe lã matsrimatsriwo katã atsu kple asi adreadre, kple lã tsitsriwo atsu kple asi eveveve.

ref: Also take seven pairs of every kind of bird with you. Do this so there will always be animals and birds on the earth.
text: Nenema ke naɖe dziƒoxe dzodzoewo hã atsu kple asi adreadre, bena woƒe ƒome natsi agbe le anyigba dzi.

ref: Seven days from now I will send rain that will last for
text: Elabena esusɔ ŋkeke adre mana tsi nadza ɖe anyigba dzi ŋkeke blane kple zã hã blane, eye matsrɔ̃ nusianu, si mewɔ ɖe anyigba dzi.

ref: He obeyed God and took a male and a female of each kind of animal and bird into the boat with him.
text: woyi ɖe Noa gbɔ atsu kple asi eveveve le aɖakaʋu la me, abe alesi Mawu de se na Noa ene.

ref: Seven days later a flood began to cover the earth.
text: Eye wòva me le ŋkeke adreagbe bena, tsi dza ɖe anyigba la dzi. Tsiɖɔɖɔ dze gɔme.

ref: The water under the earth started gushing out everywhere, the sky opened like windows, and rain poured down for
text: Eye tsi dzi ɖe anyigba dzi ŋkeke blane kple zã blane sɔŋ.

ref: On that day Noah and his wife went into the boat with their three sons, Shem, Ham, and Japheth, and their wives.
text: Le ŋkeke ma dzi tututu Noa kple viaŋutsuwo: Sem, Xam kple Yafet kple Noa srɔ̃ hekpe ɖe viaŋutsuawo srɔ̃ etɔ̃awo ŋuti, woyi ɖe aɖakaʋu la me;

ref: Noah took a male and a female of every living creature with him,
text: Eye lãwo tsyo ƒomevi, siwo me agbegbɔgbɔ le la, woyi de Noa gbɔ eveveve le aɖakaʋu la me.

ref: just as God had told him to do. And when they were all in the boat, the Lord closed the door.
text: Eye lã, siwo yi ɖe egbɔ la, wonye atsu kple asi; lã vovovowo yi ɖe egbɔ, abe alesi Mawu ɖe se nɛ ene. Eye Yehowa do ʋɔ ɖe enu.

ref: Finally, the mighty flood was so deep that even the highest mountain peaks were about seven meters below the surface of the water.
text: Eye tsia dɔ gbɔ to kɔkɔwo ta me abɔklugui wuiatɔ̃; ale wɔ, eye towo katã bu de tsia me.

ref: The Lord destroyed everything that breathed. Nothing was left alive except Noah and the others in the boat.
text: Nusianu, si nɔ anyigba dzi, tso amewo dzi vaseɖe aƒemelãwo kple nu tatawo kpakple dziƒoxe dzodzoewo dzi, wotsrɔ̃ wo ɖa le anyigba dzi; negbe Noa kple nusiwo le egbɔ le aɖakaʋua me koe tsi agbe.

ref: A hundred fifty days later, the water started going down.
text: Eye tsia le ɖɔɖɔm ɖe anyigba dzi ŋkeke alafa ɖeka kple blaatɔ̃ sɔŋ.

ref: God did not forget about Noah and the animals with him in the boat. So God made a wind blow, and the water started going down.
text: Eye Mawu ɖo ŋku Noa kple gbemelã kple aƒemelã, siwo katã le egbɔ le aɖakaʋua me la dzi, eye Mawu na ya ƒo ɖe anyigba dzi, ale bena tsia de asi miemie me.

ref: God stopped up the places where the water had been gushing out from under the earth. He also closed up the sky, and the rain stopped.
text: Eye anyigbatu me tsiwo nu tso, eye dziƒo nu tu; ale wɔ tsi la ke.

ref: Then on the seventeenth day of the seventh month of the year, the boat came to rest somewhere in the Ararat mountains.
text: Le ɣleti adrelia ƒe ŋkeke wuiadrelia dzi aɖakaʋu la va xa ɖe Ararat-towo dometɔ ɖeka ta me.

ref: The water kept going down, and the mountain tops could be seen on the first day of the tenth month.
text: Eye tsia le miemiem ʋeʋeʋe vaseɖe ɣleti ewolia dzi; le ɣleti ewolia ƒe ŋkeke gbãtɔ dzi towo ƒe tagbɔ dze.

ref: Forty days later Noah opened a window to send out a raven, but it kept flying around until the water had dried up.
text: Eye wòʋu nu le akpaviãa ɖe nu ɖo ɖa, eye wòle dzodzom tso afi yi afi, vaseɖe esime tsia mie le anyigba dzi vo.

ref: Noah wanted to find out if the water had gone down, so he sent out a dove.
text: Tete wòʋu nu le ahɔnea ɖe nu ɖo ɖa, ne yeakpɔ bena, tsia mie vo le anyigba blibo la katã dzi hã?

ref: Deep water was still everywhere, and when the dove could not find a place to land, it flew back to the boat. Then Noah held out his hand and helped it back in.
text: Ke esi ahɔne la mekpɔ afisi wòadze ɖo o la, egatrɔ va egbɔ le aɖakaʋu la me, elabena tsi le anyigba blibo la katã dzi, eye wòdo asi ɖa lée de aɖakaʋu la me.

ref: Seven days later Noah sent the dove out again.
text: Emegbe wòlala ŋkeke adre, eye wòʋu nu le ahɔnea nu le aɖakaʋu la me.

ref: It returned in the evening, holding in its beak a green leaf from an olive tree. Noah knew the water was finally going down.
text: Eye ahɔne la va egbɔ le fiẽ me, eye kpɔ ɖa, amitiɖɔmee le enu. Azɔ Noa dze sii bena, tsia mie le anyigba dzi.

ref: He waited seven more days before sending the dove out again, and this time it did not return.
text: Eye wògalala ŋkeke adre bubu, eye wògaʋu nu le ahɔnea nu, ke megado va egbɔ o.

ref: By the twenty-seventh day of the second month, the earth was completely dry.
text: Le ɣleti evelia ƒe ŋkeke blave vɔ adrelia dzi anyigba dzi ƒu keŋkeŋ. Noa me nu na Mawu.

ref: God said to Noah,
text: Eye Mawu ƒo nu kple Noa gblɔ bena:

ref: “You, your wife, your sons, and your daughters-in-law may now leave the boat.
text: Do go le aɖakaʋu la me, wò ŋutɔ kple srɔ̃wo kple viwòŋutsuwo hekpe ɖe viwòŋutsuawo srɔ̃wo ŋuti!

ref: After Noah and his family had left the boat,
text: Tete Noa do go, eye viaŋutsuwo kple srɔ̃a kpakple viaŋutsuawo srɔ̃wo hã kpe ɖe eŋuti.

ref: the living creatures left in groups of their own kind. The Lord's Promise for the Earth
text: Lãwo katã, nu tata wo katã kple xe dzodzoewo katã kpakple nusi wo katã zɔna le anyigba dzi la, wodo go le aɖakaʋu la me hamehame.

ref: Noah built an altar where he could offer sacrifices to the Lord. Then he offered on the altar one of each kind of animal and bird that could be used for a sacrifice.
text: Tete Noa ɖi vɔsamlekpui, eye wòtsɔ lã matsrimatsriwo kple xe matsrimatsriwo ƒe ɖewo na Yehowa hemè wo le vɔsamlekpui la dzi. Yehowa ƒe ŋugbedodo.

ref: The smell of the burning offering pleased the Lord, and he said: Never again will I punish the earth for the sinful things its people do. All of them have evil thoughts from the time they are young, but I will never destroy everything that breathes, as I did this time.
text: Ke esi Yehowa se ʋeʋẽ lililia la, egblɔ le eƒe dzi me bena: Nyemagaƒo fi ade anyigba akpɔ le amegbetɔ ta o. Elabena ame ƒe dzimesusu enye vɔ̃ tso ɖevime ke, eye nyemagatsrɔ̃ nu gbagbewo katã, abe alesi mewo ene o.

ref: As long as the earth remains, there will be planting and harvest, cold and heat; winter and summer, day and night.
text: Tso esia dzi, ŋkeke alesi anyigba li la, nuƒaƒã kple nuŋeŋe, viwo kple fifia, dzomeŋɔli kple pepi, ŋkeke kple zã nu matsi o.

ref: God said to Noah and his sons: I am giving you my blessing. Have a lot of children and grandchildren, so people will live everywhere on this earth.
text: Eye Mawu yra Noa kple viaŋutsuawo, eye wògblɔ na wo bena: Midzi, ne miasɔ gbe, eye miayɔ anyigba la dzi!

ref: All animals, birds, reptiles, and fish will be afraid of you. I have placed them under your control,
text: Metsɔ anyigba dzi lãwo katã kple dziƒoxe dzodzoewo katã kple nusiwo katã tana le anyigba dzi, kple tɔmelãwo katã de asi na mi, eye mianye vɔvɔ̃ kple ŋɔdzi na wo.

ref: and I have given them to you for food. From now on, you may eat them, as well as the green plants that you have always eaten.
text: Nu gbagbe, siwo katã ʋãna, nenema nu siawo katã metsɔ na mi, bena woanye miaƒe nuɖuɖu abe amagbewo ke ene.

ref: But life is in the blood, and you must not eat any meat that still has blood in it.
text: Hafi lã, si ʋu tsi lã me na la, migaɖui o!

ref: I want you and your descendants to have many children, so people will live everywhere on earth.
text: Ke miawo la midzi, ne miasɔ gbɔ, miʋã mia ɖokui le anyigba dzi, eye mikeke ta ɖe edzi! Nubabla kple anyieʋɔ.

ref: Again, God said to Noah and his sons:
text: Eye Mawu gblɔ na Noa kple viaŋutsuawo bena:

ref: I am going to make a solemn promise to you and to everyone who will live after you.
text: Kpɔ ɖa, mebia nu kpli mi kple míaƒe dzidzimeviwo

ref: I promise every living creature that the earth and those living on it will never again be destroyed by a flood.
text: Mebia nu kpli mi bena, nyemagatsrɔ̃ ŋutilã sia ŋutilã kple tsiɖɔɖɔ o; tsi magaɖe anyigba agblẽ nu le edzi akpɔ o.

ref: The rainbow that I have put in the sky will be my sign to you and to every living creature on earth. It will remind you that I will keep this promise forever.
text: Meɖo nye anyieʋɔ ɖe alilikpowo ŋu, bena wòanye nubabla, si le mía kple anyigba dome la ƒe dzesi.

ref: When I send clouds over the earth, and a rainbow appears in the sky,
text: Eye ava me bena, ne mele ƒu ƒom alilikpowo le dziƒo, eye anyieʋɔ ɖi ɖe alilikpoawo ŋu lã,

ref: I will remember my promise to you and to all other living creatures. Never again will I let floodwaters destroy all life.
text: ekema maɖo ŋku nye nubabla, si mebia kpli mi kpakple ŋutilã sia ŋutilã, siwo me agbegbɔgbɔ le la dzi, eye tsi magaɖɔ aɖe anyigba la bena, nu gbagbewo katã natsrɔ̃ o.

ref: When I see the rainbow in the sky, I will always remember the promise that I have made to every living creature.
text: Eyata ne anyieʋɔ do ɖe alilikpowo ŋu la, makpɔe ahaɖo ŋku nubabla, si le Mawu kple ŋutilã, siwo katã me agbegbɔgbɔ le le anyigba dzi dome la dzi tegbee.

ref: The rainbow will be the sign of that solemn promise. Noah and His Family
text: Eye Mawu gblɔ na Noa bena: Esia enye nubabla, si meɖo anyi le mia kple ŋutilã, siwo katã le anyigba dzi dome la ƒe dzesi.

ref: Noah and his sons, Shem, Ham, and Japheth, left the boat. Ham later had a son named Canaan.
text: Noa ƒe viŋutsu, siwo do go le aɖakaʋua me la, woawo nye: Sem, Xam kple Yafet; Xam sia enye Kanaan fofo.

ref: Noah farmed the land and was the first to plant a vineyard.
text: Eye Noa de asi agbledede me, eye wòde weingblea ɖe.

ref: One day he got drunk and was lying naked in his tent.
text: Ke esi wòno wein mu la, eɖe ama ɖe eƒe agbadɔ me.

ref: Ham entered the tent and saw him naked, then went back outside and told his brothers.
text: Eye Xam, Kanaan fofo, kpɔ fofoa ƒe ama, eye wòɖagblɔe na nɔvia eveawo le xexe.

ref: Shem and Japheth put a robe over their shoulders and walked backwards into the tent. Without looking at their father, they placed it over his body.
text: Tete Sem kple Yafet tsɔ avɔ lé ɖe abɔta hezɔ megbemegbee, eye wotsyɔe na wo fofo, eye wometrɔ kɔ kpɔ wo fofo ƒe ama o.

ref: When Noah sobered up and learned what his youngest son had done,
text: Ke esi aha la kɔ le mo na Noa, eye wòse nusi via ɖevitɔ wɔ ɖe eŋu la,

ref: he said, “I now put a curse on Canaan! He will be the lowest slave of his brothers.
text: egblɔ bena: Woaƒo fi ade Kanaan, kluviwo ƒe kluvi wòanye na nɔviawo!

ref: I ask the Lord my God to bless Shem and make Canaan his slave.
text: Eye wògagblɔbena: Woakafu Yehowa, Sem ƒe Mawu; eye Kanaan anye kluvi na wo!

ref: I pray God will give Japheth more and more land and let him take over the territory of Shem. May Canaan be his slave.”
text: Mawu ana be, Yafet ƒe nɔƒe nakeke ɖe nu, eye wòano Sem ƒe agbadɔwo me; eye Kanaan anye kluvi na wo!

ref: After the flood Shem, Ham, and Japheth had many descendants. The Descendants of Japheth
text: Esia nye Noa viŋutsuwo, Sem, Xam kple Yafet ƒe dzidzimeviwo ŋutinya: Le tsiɖɔɖɔ la megbe la wodzi ŋutsuviwo na wo.

ref: Japheth's descendants had their own languages, tribes, and land. They were Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech, and Tiras. Gomer was the ancestor of Ashkenaz, Riphath, and Togarmah. Javan was the ancestor of Elishah, Tarshish, Kittim, and Dodanim, who settled along the coast. The Descendants of Ham
text: Amesiawo me dukɔ, siwo nɔa ƒukpowo dzi la, dzɔ tso. Esiawo nye Yafet viŋutsuwo le woƒe anyigbawo, woƒe gbe vovovowo, woƒe ƒomewo kple woƒe dukɔwo nu.

ref: This completes the list of Noah's descendants. After the flood their descendants became nations and spread all over the world.
text: Esia nye Noa viŋutsuwo ƒe ƒomewo le woƒe dzidzimewo kple woƒe dukɔwo nu, eye woawo me anyigba dzi dukɔwo dzɔ tso le tsiɖɔɖɔ la megbe.

ref: At first everyone spoke the same language,
text: Ke gbe ɖeka kple nuƒoƒo ɖeka ŋugbe le amegbetɔwo katã nu.

ref: but after some of them moved from the east and settled in Babylonia,
text: Eye wòva me bena, esi wodze mo ɖo ta ɣedzeƒe gome la, wokpɔ gbadzaƒea ɖe le Sinear-nyigba dzi, eye wonɔ afima.

ref: they said: Let's build a city with a tower that reaches to the sky! We'll use hard bricks and tar instead of stone and mortar. We'll become famous, and we won't be scattered all over the world.
text: Eye wogblɔ bena: Mina míatso du, eye miatu mɔ̃ kɔkɔa ɖe, si tame nato dziƒo, ne míaƒe ŋkɔ nanyɔ, bena míagakaka ɖe anyigba blibo la katã dzi o!

ref: But when the Lord came down to look at the city and the tower,
text: Tete Yehowa ɖi va anyi, be yeakpɔ du kple mɔ̃ kɔkɔ, si amegbetɔviwo le tutum la ɖa. Mawu tɔtɔ amewo ƒe gbe.

ref: he said: These people are working together because they all speak the same language. This is just the beginning. Soon they will be able to do anything they want.
text: Eye Yehowa gblɔ bena: Kpɔ ɖa, dukɔ ɖeka wonye, eye gbe ɖeka le wo katã nu, eye esia nye woƒe nuwɔwɔ ƒe gɔmedzedze ko; azɔ nusianu, si dim wole be, woawɔ la, womado kpoe o.

ref: Let's go down and confuse them! We'll make them speak different languages, and they won't be able to understand each other.
text: Mina míaɖi ayi afima, ne míatɔtɔ woƒe gbe, bena amea ɖeke nagase nɔvia ƒe gbe o!

ref: So the people had to stop building the city, because the Lord confused their language and scattered them all over the earth. That's how the city of Babel got its name. The Descendants of Shem
text: Eyata woyɔnɛ bena Babel, le esi Yehowa tɔtɔ amegbetɔwo katã ƒe gbe le afima, eye Yehowa na wokaka tso afima ɖe anyigba blibo la katã dzi ta. Dzidzime xexlẽfia tso Sem dzi vaseɖe Abram dzi.

ref: Two years after the flood, when Shem was
text: Esi Sem dzi Arpaxsad megbe la, eganɔ agbe ƒe alafa atɔ̃, eye wòdzi ŋutsuwo kple nyɔnuwo.

ref: Arpachshad had more children and died at the age of
text: Eye esi Arpaxsad dzi Selax megbe la, eganɔ agbe ƒe alafa ene kple etɔ̃, eye wòdzi ŋutsuwo kple nyɔnuwo.

ref: Shelah had more children and died at the age of
text: Eye esi Selax dzi Eber megbe la, eganɔ agbe ƒe alafa ene kple etɔ̃, eye wòdzi ŋutsuwo kple nyɔnuwo.

ref: Eber had more children and died at the age of
text: Eye esi Eber dzi Peleg megbe la, eganɔ agbe ƒe alafa ene kple blaetɔ̃, eye wòdzi ŋutsuwo kple nyɔnuwo.

ref: Peleg had more children and died at the age of
text: Eye esi Peleg dzi Regu megbe la, eganɔ agbe ƒe alafa eve kple asieke, eye wòdzi ŋutsuwo kple nyɔnuwo.

ref: Reu had more children and died at the age of
text: Eye esi Regu dzi Serug megbe la, eganɔ agbe ƒe alafa eve kple adre, eye wòdzi ŋutsuwo kple nyɔnuwo.

ref: Serug had more children and died at the age of
text: Eye esi Serug dzi Naxor megbe la, eganɔ agbe ƒe alafa eve, eye wòdzi ŋutsuwo kple nyɔnuwo.

ref: Nahor had more children and died at the age of
text: Eye esi Naxor dzi Tarax megbe la, eganɔ agbe ƒe alafa ɖeka kple wuiasieke, eye wodzi ŋutsuwo kple nyɔnuwo.

ref: Terah decided to move from Ur to the land of Canaan. He took along Abram and Sarai and his grandson Lot, the son of Haran. But when they came to the city of Haran, they settled there instead.
text: Eye Tarax ʋu kple via Abram kple tɔgbuiyɔvia Lot, si nye Haran vi, hekpe ɖe Sarai, via Abram srɔ̃ ŋuti tso Ur le Kaldea, bene wòayi Kanaan-nyigba dzi Ke esi wova Xaran la, wònɔ afima.

ref: The Lord said to Abram: Leave your country, your family, and your relatives and go to the land that I will show you.
text: Eye Yehowa gblɔ na Abram bena: Ʋu le wò anyigba dzi kple ƒowòmeawo gbɔ kple fofowò ƒe me, eye nayi anyigba, si mafia wò la dzi!

ref: I will bless you and make your descendants into a great nation. You will become famous and be a blessing to others.
text: Mawɔ wò dukɔ gã, eye mayra wò, mana wò ŋkɔ nanyɔ, eye nazu yayra.

ref: I will bless those who bless you, but I will put a curse on anyone who puts a curse on you. Everyone on earth will be blessed because of you.
text: Mayra amesiwo yra wò, ke amesiwo doa vlo wò la, maƒo fi ade wo, eye le wò me woayra anyigba dzi meawo katã le. Abram ʋu yi Kanaan.

ref: Abram went as far as the sacred tree of Moreh in a place called Shechem. The Canaanites were still living in the land at that time,
text: Eye Abram tsa le anyigba la dzi vaseɖe Sixem kple Moreli gã la gbɔ; ke ɣemaɣi la Kanaantɔwo le anyigba la dzi. Ŋugbeɖoɖo gbãtɔ.

ref: but the Lord appeared to Abram and promised, “I will give this land to your family forever.” Abram then built an altar there for the Lord.
text: Eye Yehowa ɖe eɖokui fia Abram gblɔ nɛ bena: Wò dzidzimeviwo matsɔ anyigba sia ana. Eye wòɖi vɔsamlekpui ɖe afima na Yehowa, amesi ɖe eɖokui fiae.

ref: Abram traveled to the hill country east of Bethel and camped between Bethel and Ai, where he built another altar and worshiped the Lord.
text: Eye wòʋu tso afima yi ɖe to dzi le Betel ƒe ɣedzeƒe, eye wòtu eƒe agbadɔ ɖi; eɖe Betel ɖe ɣetoɖoƒe kple Ai ɖe ɣedzeƒe, eye wòɖi vɔsamlekpui na Yehowa heyɔ Yehowa ŋkɔ le afima.

ref: Later, Abram started out toward the Southern Desert. Abram in Egypt
text: Eye Abram le ʋuʋum le dziehe gome yim ʋeʋeʋe. Abram nye amedzro le Egipte.

ref: When the Egyptians see you, they will murder me because I am your husband. But they won't kill you.
text: Ne Egiptetɔwo akpɔ wò la, ekema woagblɔ bena, srɔ̃e sia, eye woawum; ke woana wòa natsi agbe.

ref: Please save my life by saying you are my sister.”
text: Gblɔ ko dzro bena, nɔvinyenyɔnu nènye, ne manɔ dedie le tawò, eye matsi agbe le tawò!

ref: As soon as Abram and Sarai arrived in Egypt, the Egyptians noticed how beautiful she was.
text: Eye esi Abram va Egipte la, Egiptetɔwo kpɔ nyɔnu la bena, edze tugbe ŋutɔ.

ref: The king's officials told him about her, and she was taken to his house.
text: Eye esi Farao ŋumewo kpɔe la, wokafui na Farao, eye wokplɔ nyɔnu la va Farao ƒe me.

ref: The king was good to Abram because of Sarai, and Abram was given sheep, cattle, donkeys, slaves, and camels.
text: Eye wòwɔ Abram nyuie le eta, eye wotso alẽwo, nyiwo, tedzitsuwo, kluviwo, kosiwo, tedzinɔwo kple kposɔwo nɛ.

ref: Because of Sarai, the Lord struck the king and everyone in his palace with terrible diseases.
text: Ke Yehowa ɖe fu geɖe na Farao kple eƒe aƒe katã le Sarai, Abram srɔ̃ ŋuti.

ref: Finally, the king sent for Abram and said to him, “What have you done to me? Why didn't you tell me Sarai was your wife?
text: Eye Farao yɔ Abram ve gblɔ ne bena; Nuka enye si nèwɔ ɖe ŋunye; nukaŋuti megblɔe nam bena, ye srɔ̃e wònye o mahã?

ref: Why did you make me believe she was your sister? Now I've married her. Take her and go! She's your wife.”
text: Nukaŋuti nègblɔ bena, ye nɔvinyɔnue, eye mena wòzu srɔ̃nye mahã? Ke azɔ kpɔ ɖa, meɖe asi le srɔ̃wò ŋuti na wò, kplɔe yii!

ref: So the king told his men to let Abram and Sarai take their possessions and leave.
text: Eye Farao do amewo, bena woakplɔ eya kple srɔ̃a kple etɔwo katã aɖo ɖa.

ref: Abram and Sarai took everything they owned and went to the Southern Desert. Lot went with them.
text: Ale Abram ʋu le Egipte kple srɔ̃ kple nusiwo katã le esi la, hekpe ɖe Lot ŋuti, gatrɔ va dziehe gome.

ref: Abram was very rich. He owned many cattle, sheep, and goats, and had a lot of silver and gold.
text: Eye lãwo, klosalo kple sika geɖe le Abram si taŋ.

ref: Abram moved from place to place in the Southern Desert. And finally, he went north and set up his tents between Bethel and Ai,
text: Eye wòzɔ tso dziehe gome vaseɖe Betel, vaseɖe teƒe, si wòtu eƒe agbadɔ ɖo tsã, le Betel kple Ai dome,

ref: where he had earlier camped and built an altar. There he worshiped the Lord.
text: afisi wòɖi vɔsamlekpui ɖo gbã. Eye Abram yɔ Yehowa ƒe ŋkɔ le afima.

ref: Lot, who was traveling with him, also had sheep, goats, and cattle, as well as his own family and slaves.
text: Ke Lot, amesi ʋu kple Abram hã, kpɔ alẽwo, nyiwo kple agbadɔwo.

ref: Abram said to Lot, “We are close relatives. We shouldn't argue, and our men shouldn't be fighting one another.
text: Tete Abram gblɔ na Lot bena: Megana dzre nanɔ mía kpli wò, nye lãnyilawo kple wò lãnyilawo dome o, elabena nɔviwo míenye.

ref: There is plenty of land for you to choose from. Let's separate. If you go north, I'll go south; if you go south, I'll go north.”
text: Alo menye anyigba blibo la nye esi le ŋkuwò me oa? De megbe tso gbɔnye dzro! Ne ètia mia me la, matsɔ ɖusi me, eye ne ètia ɖusi me la, matsɔ mia me.

ref: This happened before the Lord had destroyed the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah. And when Lot looked around, he saw there was plenty of water in the Jordan Valley. All the way to Zoar the valley was as green as the garden of the Lord or the land of Egypt.
text: Tete Lot tsa ŋku Yordan-nutowo katã me, eye wòkpɔ bena, tsi li ŋutɔ, elabena Yehowa metsrɔ̃ Sodom kple Gomora haɖe o, ele abe Yehowa ƒe abɔ me ene, eɖi Egipte-nyigba vaseɖe Zoar ke.

ref: So Lot chose the whole Jordan Valley for himself, and as he started out toward the east, he and Abram separated.
text: Eye Lot tia Yordan-nutowo me katã na eɖokui, eye Lot ʋu yi ɖe ɣedzeƒe gome; ale nɔvi kple nɔvi dome klã.

ref: Abram stayed in the land of Canaan. But Lot settled near the cities of the valley and put up his tents not far from Sodom,
text: Abram nɔ Kanaan-nyigba dzi; ke Lot nɔ nuto ma ƒe duwo me le eƒe agbadɔ hom le tutum, vaseɖe esime wòɖo Sodom.

ref: where the people were evil and sinned terribly against the Lord. Abram Moves to Hebron
text: Ke Sodomtɔwo nye ame baɖawo, eye woda vo geɖe ɖe Yehowa ŋuti. Mawu gado ŋugbe na Abram.

ref: After Abram and Lot had gone their separate ways, the Lord said to Abram: Look around to the north, south, east, and west.
text: Esi Lot klã le Abram gbɔ megbe la, Yehowa gblɔ na Abram bena: Tsa ŋku tso afisi nele, eye nakpɔ anyiehe, dziehe, ɣedzeƒe kple ɣetoɖoƒe ɖa.

ref: I will give you and your family all the land you can see. It will be theirs forever!
text: Elabena anyigba blibo la katã, si kpam nèle la, matsɔe ana wò kple wò dzidzimeviwo tegbetegbe.

ref: I will give you more descendants than there are specks of dust on the earth, and someday it will be easier to count those specks of dust than to count your descendants.
text: Eye mana wò dzidzimeviwo nasɔ gbɔ abe ʋuʋudedi ene ale be, ne amea ɖe ate ŋu axlẽ ʋuʋudedi la, eya koe ate ŋu axlẽ wo dzidzimeviwo hã.

ref: Now walk back and forth across the land, because I am going to give it to you.
text: Tsi tre natsa, eye nakpɔ anyigba la ƒe didime kple eƒe kekeme, elabena wòe màtsɔe ana!

ref: Abram took down his tents and went to live near the sacred trees of Mamre at Hebron, where he built an altar in honor of the Lord.
text: Eye Abram le eƒe agbadɔ hom le tutum, le ŋgɔgbe yimii, eye wòva nɔ Mamre ƒe ati gã gbɔ le Xebron-nutowo me, eye wòɖi vɔsamlekpui ɖe afima na Yehowa.

ref: About this time, King Amraphel of Babylonia, King Arioch of Ellasar, King Chedorlaomer of Elam, and King Tidal of Goiim
text: Eye wòva me le ŋkeke mawo me bena, Amrafel, Sinear-fia, Ariok, Elasar-fia, Kedorlaomer, Elam-fia kple Tideal, Goyim-fia,

ref: attacked King Bera of Sodom, King Birsha of Gomorrah, King Shinab of Admah, King Shemeber of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela, a city also known as Zoar.
text: wova wɔa aʋa kple Bera, Sodom-fia, Birsa, Gomora-fia, Sineab, Adma-fia, Semeber, Zeboyim-fia, kple Bela- alo Zoar-fia.

ref: A year later King Chedorlaomer and his allies attacked and defeated the Rephaites in Ashteroth-Karnaim, the Zuzites in Ham, and the Emites in Shaveh-Kiriathaim.
text: Le ƒe wuienelia me Kedorlaomer kple fia, siwo kpe ɖe eŋuti la, va si Refaitɔwo le Astarot-Kamayim, Zuzitɔwo le Ham, Emitɔwo le Kiryatayim-gbadzaƒe

ref: They also defeated the Horites in the hill country of Edom, as far as El-Paran, near the desert.
text: kple Xoritɔwo le woƒe to Seir dzi vaseɖe El-Paran, si te ɖe gbedzi lɔƒo.

ref: They went back to the city of Enmishpat, better known as Kadesh. Then they captured all the land that belonged to the Amalekites, and they defeated the Amorites who were living in Hazazon-Tamar.
text: Emegbe wòtrɔ va En-Mispat, si enye Kades, eye wòsi Amalekitɔwo katã kple Amoritɔ, siwo nɔ Xazazon-Tamar la hã.

ref: was full of tar pits, and when the troops from Sodom and Gomorrah started running away, some of them fell into the pits. Others escaped to the hill country.
text: Ke aŋɔʋe geɖewo le Sidim-gbadzaƒe la. Esi Sodom-kple Gomora-fia wòsi la, woge ɖe wo me; ke ame mamleawo si yi to dzi.

ref: Their enemies took everything of value from Sodom and Gomorrah, including their food supplies.
text: Eye woha Sodom kple Gomoratɔwo ƒe kesinɔnuwo kple nuɖuɖuwo katã hedzoe.

ref: They also captured Abram's nephew Lot, who lived in Sodom. They took him and his possessions and then left.
text: Eye wole Lot, Abram ƒe tɔgãyovi, amesi nɔ Sodom hã, eye woha eƒe kesinɔnuwo dzoe. Abram ɖe Lot.

ref: At this time Abram the Hebrew was living near the oaks that belonged to Mamre the Amorite. Mamre and his brothers Eshcol and Aner were Abram's friends. Someone who had escaped from the battle told Abram
text: Tete amea ɖe si va gblɔe na Hebritɔ Abram, si le Amoritɔ Mamre, Eskol kple Aner nɔvi ƒe ali gãwo te la. Ame siawoe bia nu kple Abram.

ref: that his nephew Lot had been taken away. Three hundred and eighteen of Abram's servants were fighting men, so he took them and followed the enemy as far north as the city of Dan.
text: Azɔ esi Abram se bena, woɖe aboyo nɔvia la, eɖe eƒemevi alafa etɔ̃ kple wuienyi, amesiwo nya aʋawɔwɔ la, eye wòti wo yome vaseɖe Dan.

ref: That night, Abram divided up his troops, attacked from all sides, and won a great victory. But some of the enemy escaped to the town of Hobah north of Damascus,
text: Eye eya ŋutɔ kple eƒe ameawo dze aʋa va fiti wo le zã me hesi wo, eye wonyã wo vaseɖe Xoba le Damasko ƒe anyiehe.

ref: and Abram went after them. He brought back his nephew Lot, together with Lot's possessions and the women and everyone else who had been captured. Abram Is Blessed by Melchizedek
text: Eye wògaxɔ kesinɔnuawo katã kple nɔvia Lot hã le wo si hekpe ɖe eƒe kesinɔnuawo, nyɔnuawo kple ameha la ŋuti.

ref: Abram returned after he had defeated King Chedorlaomer and the other kings. Then the king of Sodom went to meet Abram in Shaveh Valley, which is also known as King's Valley.
text: Azɔ esi woɖu Kedorlaomer kple fia, siwo kpe ɖe eŋuti dzi hetrɔ gbɔna va aƒe la, Sodom-fia va kpee le Sawebali me, si woyɔna be fiabali me la me. Melxizedek yra Abram.

ref: King Melchizedek of Salem was a priest of God Most High. He brought out some bread and wine
text: Ke Melxizedek, Salem-fia, tsɔ abolo kple wein vɛ; ame sia enye Mawu dziƒoʋitɔ ƒe nunɔla.

ref: and said to Abram: “I bless you in the name of God Most High, Creator of heaven and earth.
text: Eye wòyrae gblɔ bena Mawu dziƒoʋitɔ, si wɔ dziƒo kple anyigba, nayra Abram!

ref: All praise belongs to God Most High for helping you defeat your enemies.” Then Abram gave Melchizedek a tenth of everything.
text: Eye woakafu Mawu dziƒoʋĩtɔ, si tso wò futɔwo ɖe asi na wò la! Tete Abram tso nuawo katã ƒe ewolia ne.

ref: The king of Sodom said to Abram, “All I want are my people. You can keep everything else.”
text: Eye Sodom-fia gblɔ na Abram bena: Tsɔ ameawo nam, ne kesinɔnuawo nasusɔ na wò!

ref: Abram answered: The Lord God Most High made the heavens and the earth. And I have promised him
text: Ke Abram gblɔ na Sodom-fia bena. Meta Yehowa, Mawu dziƒoʋitɔ, si wɔ dziƒo kple anyigba la

ref: that I won't keep anything of yours, not even a sandal strap or a piece of thread. Then you can never say that you are the one who made me rich.
text: bena, nyemaxɔ naneke atso nusiwo katã nye tɔwo la me o, ka loo, alo atokotaka gɔ̃ hã o, bena nagagblɔ bena, yee wɔ Abram wòzu kesinɔtɔ o!

ref: Let my share be the food that my men have eaten. But Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre went with me, so give them their share of what we brought back.
text: Nyemele naneke dim o, negbe nusi ɖekakpuiawo ɖu, kple ŋutsu, siwo yi kplim, Aner, Eskol kple Mamre ƒe gome la ko woawo naxɔ wo tɔ gome.

ref: Later the Lord spoke to Abram in a vision, “Abram, don't be afraid! I will protect you and reward you greatly.”
text: Le nya siawo megbe la Abram se Yehowa ƒe nya le ŋutega me bena Abram, megavɔ̃ o; nye enye wò akpoxɔnu; wò fetu sɔ gbɔ ŋutɔ.

ref: But Abram answered, “Lord All-Powerful, you have given me everything I could ask for, except children. And when I die, Eliezer of Damascus will get all I own.
text: Tete Abram gblɔ bena: Aƒetɔ Yehowa, esi vikoe le wɔyem, eye ye nuwo domenyila enye Damaskotɔ Eliezer la, nuka gɔ̃ natsɔ nama?

ref: You have not given me any children, and this servant of mine will inherit everything.”
text: Eye Abram gblɔ bena: Mèna vim o, eye kpɔ ɖa, nye aƒedzikpɔla anyi ye dome.

ref: The Lord replied, “No, he won't! You will have a son of your own, and everything you have will be his.”
text: Ke Yehowa gblɔ nɛ bena: Menye eya anyi wò dome o; boŋ amesi nava dzi la, eya anyi wò dome. Abram ƒe xɔse.

ref: Then the Lord took Abram outside and said, “Look at the sky and see if you can count the stars. That's how many descendants you will have.”
text: Eye wòkplɔe yi ɖe xexe gblɔ nɛ bena: Kpɔ dziƒo ɖa, eye naxlẽ ɣletiviwo, ne ate ŋu axlẽ wo la. Eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Nenema wò ƒome anɔ.

ref: Abram believed the Lord, so the Lord was pleased with him and accepted him. The Lord Makes Another Promise to Abram
text: Eye wòxɔ Yehowa dzi se, eye wòbui nɛ dzɔdzɔenyenyee. Mawu bia nu kple Abram.

ref: The Lord said to Abram, “I brought you here from Ur in Chaldea, and I gave you this land.”
text: Eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Nye enye Yehowa, si kplɔ wò tso Ur le Kaldea, bena matsɔ anyigba sia na wò, ne wòanye wò domenyinu.

ref: Abram asked, “Lord God, how can I know the land will be mine?”
text: Ke egblɔ nɛ bena: Aƒetɔ Yehowa, nuka me madze sii le bena, màtsɔe anyi domee mahã?

ref: Then the Lord told him, “Bring me a three-year-old cow, a three-year-old female goat, a three-year-old ram, a dove, and a young pigeon.”
text: Eye wòɖo ŋu nɛ bena: Lé nyinɔ, si xɔ ƒe etɔ̃, gbɔnɔ, si xɔ ƒe etɔ, agbo, si xɔ ƒe etɔ̃, akpakpa kple ahɔne ve nam!

ref: Abram obeyed the Lord. Then he cut the animals in half and laid the two halves of each animal opposite each other on the ground. But he did not cut the doves and pigeons in half.
text: Tete wotsɔ nu siawo katã vɛ nɛ hefe wo me, eye wòɖo akpa afafãawo ɖe agbaka eve me ɖewo nɔewo gbɔ; ke mefe xeawo ya me o.

ref: And when birds came down to eat the animals, Abram chased them away.
text: Tete xe ƒonuwo va dze ɖe lãwo dzi, ke Abram nyã wo ɖa.

ref: As the sun was setting, Abram fell into a deep sleep, and everything became dark and frightening.
text: Esi ɣe le to ɖom la, Abram dɔ alɔ̃ yi me ʋĩ, eye kpɔ ɖa, vɔvɔ̃ lée, eye tsizi do ɖee.

ref: Four generations later, your descendants will return here and take this land, because only then will the people who live here be so sinful that they deserve to be punished.
text: Hafi wò dzidzime enelia agatrɔ ava afisia, elabena Amoritɔwo ƒe vodada mesɔ gbɔ vɔ haɖe o.

ref: Sometime after sunset, when it was very dark, a smoking cooking pot and a flaming fire passed between the two halves of each animal.
text: Eye wòva ve me bena, esime ɣe ɖo to, eye viviti do la, kpɔ ɖa, dzudzɔ le dodom le kpɔa ɖe me kɔlikɔli, eye dzo ƒe ade le dzi dem le lãwo domee.

ref: At that time the Lord made an agreement with Abram and told him: I will give your descendants the land east of the Shihor River on the border of Egypt as far as the Euphrates River.
text: Le ŋkeke ma dzi Yehowa bla nu kple Abram gblɔ bena: Wò dzidzimeviwo matsɔ anyigba sia, si tso Egipte-tɔsisi la nu vaseɖe Frat-tɔsisi gã la nu ana:

ref: the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Rephaites,
text: Xititɔwo, Perizitɔwo, Refaitɔwo,

ref: the Amorites, the Canaanites, the Girgashites, and the Jebusites.
text: Amoritɔwo, Kanaanitɔwo, Girgasitɔwo kple Yebusitɔwo.

ref: Abram's wife Sarai had not been able to have any children. But she owned a young Egyptian slave woman named Hagar,
text: Eye Abram srɔ̃ Sarai medzi vi nɛ kpɔ o. Ke Egipte-nyɔnua ɖe, si woyɔna be Hagar la, nye eƒe kosi.

ref: and Sarai said to Abram, “The Lord has not given me any children. Sleep with my slave, and if she has a child, it will be mine.” Abram agreed,
text: Eye Sarai gblɔ na Abram bena: Kpɔ ɖa, Yehowa xe nye vidzidɔ, eyata dɔ kple nye kosi la, ɖewohĩ maxɔ mamayɔvi mahĩ? Eye Abram ɖo to Sarai ƒe gbe.

ref: and Sarai gave him Hagar to be his wife. This happened after Abram had lived in the land of Canaan for ten years.
text: Le Abram ƒe Kanaan-nyigba dzi nɔnɔ ƒe ewo megbe la, Sarai, Abram srɔ̃, tsɔ eƒe kosi, Egipte-nyɔnu Hagar, na srɔ̃a Abram wòɖe.

ref: Later, when Hagar knew she was going to have a baby, she became proud and treated Sarai hatefully.
text: Eye wòdɔ kple Hagar, eye wòtɔ fu. Ke esi wòkpɔ bena, yefɔ fu la, eva eƒe aƒenɔ kpɔ ge ɖe ƒomevii.

ref: Then Sarai said to Abram, “It's all your fault! I gave you my slave woman, but she has been hateful to me ever since she found out she was pregnant. You have done me wrong, and you will have to answer to the Lord for this.”
text: Eye Sarai gblɔ na Abram bena: Nu manyomanyo, si teƒe kpɔm mele la, etso gbɔwò. Nye ŋutɔ metsɔ nye kosi na wò; ke azɔ esi wòkpɔ bena, yefɔ fu ko la, eva kpɔ ye ge ɖe ƒomevii. Yehowa nadrɔ̃ ʋɔnu le mía kpli wò dome!

ref: Abram said, “All right! She's your slave—do whatever you want with her.” Then Sarai began treating Hagar so harshly that she finally ran away.
text: Tete Abram gblɔ na Sarai bena: Kpɔ ɖa, wò kosi la le asiwò me, wɔ nusianu, si nyo ŋuwò la, ɖo eŋu faa! Tete Sarai bɔbɔe ɖe anyi; eyata wòsi le egbɔ. Hagar si, eye wògatrɔ gbɔ. Hagar dzi Ismael.

ref: Hagar stopped to rest at a spring in the desert on the road to Shur. While she was there, the angel of the Lord came to her
text: Ke Yehowa ƒe dɔla kpɔe le tsivudoa ɖe, si le gbe dzi le Sur-mɔ dzi la to.

ref: and asked, “Hagar, where have you come from, and where are you going?” She answered, “I'm running away from Sarai, my owner.”
text: Eye wògblɔ nɛ bena; Hagar, Sarai ƒe kosi, afika nètso, eye afika néyina? Eɖo ŋu bena: Ɖe mesi le nye aƒenɔ Sarai gbɔ.

ref: The angel said, “Go back to Sarai and be her slave.
text: Eye Yehowa ƒe ɖɔla la gblɔ nɛ bena: Trɔ yi wò aƒenɔ gbɔ, eye nabɔbɔ ɖokuiwò ɖe ete!

ref: I will give you a son, who will be called Ishmael, because I have heard your cry for help. And someday I will give you so many descendants that no one will be able to count them all.
text: Yehowa ƒe dɔla la gagblɔ nɛ bena: Kpɔ ɖa, èfɔ fu, eye àdzi ŋutsuvi, eye nayɔ eƒe ŋkɔ bena Ismael; elabena Yehowa se wò konyi, si nèfa le wò fukpekpe la ŋuti

ref: But your son will live far from his relatives; he will be like a wild donkey, fighting everyone, and everyone fighting him.”
text: Awɔ nu abe lu ene, akɔ asi dzi ɖe amesiame ŋu, eye amesiame akɔ asi dzi ɖe eya hã ŋu, eye wòatigi nɔvia wo katã ɖo.

ref: Hagar thought, “Have I really seen God and lived to tell about it?” So from then on she called him, “The God Who Sees Me.”
text: Tete wòyɔ Yehowa, amesi ƒo nu kplii la ƒe ŋkɔ bena: Wòe nye Mawu nusianu kpɔla! Elabena egblɔ bena: Alo menye amesi le kpɔyem la yome mekpɔ ɖa vava oa?

ref: That's why people call the well between Kadesh and Bered, “The Well of the Living One Who Sees Me.”
text: Eyata woyɔ vudo ma bena: Agbetɔ, si le kpɔyem la ƒe vudo; ele Kades kple Bared dome.

ref: I will keep my solemn promise to you and give you more descendants than can be counted.”
text: Eye nye nubabla nanɔ mia kpli wò dome, eye mana nasɔ gbɔ ɖe edzi.

ref: Abram bowed with his face to the ground, and God said:
text: Tete Abram tsyɔ mo anyi, eye Mawu ƒo nu kplii bena:

ref: I promise that you will be the father of many nations. So now I'm changing your name from Abram to Abraham.
text: Eyata womagayɔ wò bena Abram akpɔ o, ke boŋ Abraham anye wò ŋkɔ. Elabena meɖo wò dukɔ geɖewo fofoe.

ref: I will give you a lot of descendants, and they will become great nations. Some of them will even be kings.
text: Eye mana nadzi ɖe dzi ŋutɔ, eye dukɔwo kple fiawo adzɔ tso mewo.

ref: I will always keep the promise I have made to you and your descendants, because I am your God and their God.
text: Eye maɖo nye nubabla anyi le mía kpli wò kple megbewòviwo katã dome, eye wòanye nubabla mavɔ, bena manye wò ŋutɔ kple megbewòviwo ƒe Mawu tegbee.

ref: I will give you and them the land in which you are now a foreigner. I will give the whole land of Canaan to your family forever, and I will be their God.
text: Eye matsɔ Kanaan-nyigba blibo la katã, si dzi nènye amedzro le la, na wò, be wòanye wò ŋutɔ kple megbewòviwo tɔ tegbee, eye manye Mawu na wo. Aʋatsotso nubabla.

ref: Abraham, you and all future members of your family must promise to obey me.
text: Eye Mawu gblɔ na Abraham bena: Ke wò la lé ye nubabla la me ɖe asi; wò ŋutɔ kple megbewòviwo katã milé ye nubabla la me ɖe asi!

ref: As the sign that you are keeping this promise, you must circumcise every man and boy in your family.
text: Woaɖe miaƒe aʋanuyiwo ɖa, eye esia anye mia kple mia dome nubabla la ƒe dzesi.

ref: Any man who isn't circumcised hasn't kept his part of the promise and cannot be one of my people.
text: Eye ne ŋutsua ɖe ti, si womeɖe aʋanuyi ɖa nɛ o la, woaɖe nenem bolobolotɔ sia ɖa le eƒe dukɔ la dome, elabena etu nye nubabla la. Sarai xɔ ŋkɔ bena Sara.

ref: Abraham, from now on your wife's name will be Sarah instead of Sarai.
text: Eye Mawu gagblɔ na Abraham bena: Megayɔ srɔ̃wò Sarai bena Sarai azɔ o, ke boŋ Sara anye eƒe ŋkɔ.

ref: I will bless her, and you will have a son by her. She will become the mother of nations, and some of her descendants will even be kings.
text: Mayrae, eye mana wòadzi ŋutsuvi hã na wò. Mayrae ale bena, wòava nye dukɔwo kple dukɔwo dziɖulawo dada.

ref: Then he asked God, “Why not let Ishmael inherit what you have promised me?”
text: Eye Abraham gblɔ na Mawu bena: Ismael netsi agbe na wò ko! Mawu ɖo Isak ŋugbe na Abraham.

ref: But God answered: No! You and Sarah will have a son. His name will be Isaac, and I will make an everlasting promise to him and his descendants.
text: Tete Mawu gblɔ bena: Nyateƒe! Srɔ̃wò Sara adzi ŋutsuvi na wò, eye nayɔ eƒe ŋkɔ bena Isak, eye mabia nu kple eya kpakple emegbeviwo tegbee.

ref: However, I have heard what you asked me to do for Ishmael, and so I will also bless him with many descendants. He will be the father of twelve princes, and I will make his family a great nation.
text: Ke le Ismael to gome la, mese wò nyawo. Kpɔ ɖa, mayrae, eye mana wòadzi, eye wòasɔ gbɔ ɖe dzi. Adzi fia wuieve, eye mawɔe wòazu dukɔ gãa ɖe.

ref: But your son Isaac will be born about this time next year, and the promise I am making to you and your family will be for him and his descendants forever.
text: Ke mabia nu kple Isak, amesi Sara adzi na wò le ƒe, si gbɔna ƒe woalèwoɣi la.

ref: God finished speaking to Abraham and then left.
text: Esi Mawu wu eƒe nuƒoƒo nu kplii la, edzo yi dzi le Abraham gbɔ.

ref: On that same day Abraham obeyed God by circumcising Ishmael. Abraham was also circumcised, and so were all the other men and boys in his household, including his servants and slaves. He was
text: Eye eƒe me ŋutsuwo katã, amesiwo wodzi le eƒe me, kple dzrovi, siwo woƒle kple ga la, wotso aʋa kpe ɖe eŋu.

ref: One hot summer afternoon while Abraham was sitting by the entrance to his tent near the sacred trees of Mamre, the Lord appeared to him.
text: Eye Yehowa ɖe eɖokui fiae le Mamre ƒe ati gãwo gbɔ, le esime wòbɔbɔ nɔ eƒe agbadɔ nu le ŋdɔ me.

ref: Abraham looked up and saw three men standing nearby. He quickly ran to meet them, bowed with his face to the ground,
text: Esi wòfɔ mo te la, kasia ekpɔ ŋutsu etɔ̃ le tsitre le eŋgɔ ɖa; eye esi wòkpɔ wo ko la, eƒu du tso eƒe agbadɔ nu yi ɖakpe wo, eye wobɔbɔ tsyɔ mo anyi.

ref: and said, “Please come to my home where I can serve you.
text: Tete wògblɔ bena; Aƒetɔ, ne eve nunye la, ekema megato wò dɔla ŋu yi o.

ref: I'll have some water brought, so you can wash your feet, then you can rest under a tree.
text: Woaku tsi via ɖe ve, ne miaklɔ miaƒe afɔwo ŋuti; emegbe la miagbɔ ɖe eme vie,

ref: Let me get you some food to give you strength before you leave. I would be honored to serve you.” “Thank you very much,” they answered. “We accept your offer.”
text: eye matsɔ abolo via ɖe vɛ, ne míado ŋusẽ mia ɖokui hafi adzo; elabena esia ta mieva to miaƒe dɔla gbɔ ɖo. Eye woxɔ dzi bena: Wɔ, abe alesi negblɔ ene!

ref: Abraham went quickly to his tent and said to Sarah, “Hurry! Get a large sack of flour and make some bread.”
text: Tete Abraham ƒu ɖu yi ɖe Sara gbɔ le agbadɔ me, eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Wɔ kaba, ku wɔ memee asiʋlo etɔ̃a ɖe, nablui, eye natsɔe atɔ tatalii.

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Eye Abraham ƒu ɖu yi ɖe nyiwo dome, eye wòlé nyivi nyuia ɖe tsɔ na eƒe dɔla wòɖawu enumake, eye wòmee wòbi nyuie.

ref: He then served his guests some yogurt and milk together with the meat. While they were eating, he stood near them under the tree,
text: Eye wòtsɔ notsi kpekẽtɔ kple notsi tsrolotɔ hekpe ɖe nyilã memee la ŋu va ɖo wo kɔme; ke eya ŋutɔ tsitre ɖe wo gbɔ le atia te, esime wole nua ɖum. Yehowa gado Isak ŋugbe na Abraham.

ref: and they asked, “Where's your wife Sarah?” “She is right there in the tent,” Abraham answered.
text: Eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Afika srɔ̃wò Sara le? Eɖo ŋu bena: Ele agbadɔ me.

ref: Abraham and Sarah were very old, and Sarah was well past the age for having children.
text: Eye Abraham kple Sara wotsi xɔ ƒe geɖe ŋutɔ, ale bena Sara megaléa gbe gɔ̃ hã o.

ref: So she laughed and said to herself, “Now that I am worn out and my husband is old, will I really know such happiness?”
text: Tete Sara ko nu ɖe dɔ me gblɔ bena: Esi metsi lé ayi ale la, ɖe nu agavivi nunyea? Eye ye aƒetɔ hã ku amegã ɖeɖi!

ref: The Lord asked Abraham, “Why did Sarah laugh? Does she doubt that she can have a child in her old age?
text: Tete Yehowa gblɔ na Abraham bena: Nukaŋuti Sara le nu kom le susum bena: Nye amesi ku nyagã la, magate ŋu adzi via?

ref: I am the Lord! There is nothing too difficult for me. I'll come back next year at the time I promised, and Sarah will already have a son.”
text: Ɖe nane sesẽ na Yehowa? Le ƒe, si gbɔna ƒe woleawoɣi la, magato ava gbɔwò, eye ŋutsuvi anɔ Sara si.

ref: Sarah was so frightened that she lied and said, “I didn't laugh.” “Yes, you did!” he answered. Abraham Prays for Sodom
text: Tete Sara sẽ nu gblɔ bena: Nyemeko nu o; elabena ele vɔvɔ̃m. Ke egblɔ ne bena: O, èko nu tso! Sodom gbegblẽ gbɔgblɔɖi.

ref: When the three men got ready to leave, they looked down toward Sodom, and Abraham walked part of the way with them.
text: Esia megbe la ŋutsuawo dze mɔ tso afima, eye wokpɔ Sodom le abu me ɖa; ke Abraham yi kpli wo, be yeaɖo wo ɖa.

ref: The Lord said to himself, “I should tell Abraham what I am going to do,
text: Eye Yehowa gblɔ bena: Ɖe maɣla nusi wɔ ge mala la, ɖe Abraham,

ref: since his family will become a great and powerful nation that will be a blessing to all the other nations on earth.
text: esi wònye Abraham ava nye dukɔ gã sẽŋua ɖe, eye woayra anyigba dzi dukɔwo katã le eme laa?

ref: I have chosen him to teach his family to obey me forever and to do what is right and fair. If they do, I will give Abraham many descendants, just as I promised.”
text: Elabena metiae, bena wòade se na emegbeviwo kple eƒe me katã bena, woalé ŋku ɖe Yehowa ƒe mɔ ŋuti, eye woawɔ nu dzɔdzɔe kple nu nyui, bena ne nusi ŋugbe Yehowa do la, nava me na Abraham.

ref: ?” “Because of them, I won't destroy the city,” was the Lord's answer.
text: Tete Yehowa gblɔ bena: Dodo ɖe Sodom kple Gomora ta sɔ gbɔ akpa, eye woƒe dzidadawo tso me akpa.

ref: Now I am going down to see for myself if those people really are that bad. If they aren't, I would like to know.”
text: Eyata maɖi ayi, ne makpɔ bena, nya, siwo katã sem mele le wo ŋuti la, le eme loo, alo mele eme o, ne manya. Abraham ɖo kuku ɖe Sodom ta.

ref: The men turned and started toward Sodom. But the Lord stayed with Abraham.
text: Esia megbe la ŋutsuawo trɔ yi Sodom; ke Abraham gatɔ ɖe Yehowa gbɔ ko.

ref: And Abraham asked him, “Lord, when you destroy the evil people, are you also going to destroy those who are good?
text: Eye Abraham te ɖe eŋu gblɔ bena: Ɖe atsrɔ̃ ame dzɔdzɔewo kple ame vɔ̃ɖiwo siaa mahã?

ref: You surely wouldn't let them be killed when you destroy the evil ones. You are the judge of all the earth, and you do what is right.”
text: Esia nede megbe xaa tso gbɔwò be, nagawu ame dzɔdzɔe kple ame vɔ̃ɖi siaa zi ɖeka, eye ame dzɔdzɔe nanɔ abe ame vɔ̃ɖi ene o! Esia nede megbe xaa tso gbɔwò! Ɖe xexeame ƒe ʋɔnudrɔ̃la madrɔ̃ ʋɔnu dzɔdzɔe oa?

ref: Abraham answered, “I am nothing more than the dust of the earth. Please forgive me, Lord, for daring to speak to you like this.
text: Abraham xɔ dzi gblɔ bena: Kpɔ ɖa, nye amesi nye anyi kple afi ko la, meɖo dzi be, maƒo nu kpli wò, Aƒetɔ!

ref: Then Abraham said, “I don't have any right to ask you, Lord, but what would you do if you find only
text: Eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Kpɔ ɖa, medo dzi be, maƒo nu kpli wò Aƒetɔ. Ɖewohi woakpɔ ame blave le eme. Eɖo ŋu nɛ be: Le ame blave ta nyematsrɔ̃ wo o.

ref: Finally, Abraham said, “Please don't get angry, Lord, if I speak just once more. Suppose you find only
text: Eye wògblɔ be: Aƒetɔ, megabi dzi o loo, ne magaƒo nu zi ɖeka pɛ ko. Ɖewohĩ woakpɔ ame ewo le eme. Eɖo ŋu nɛ be: Le ame ewo ta nyematsrɔ̃ wo o.

ref: After speaking with Abraham, the Lord left, and Abraham went back home.
text: Ke esi Yehowa wu nuƒoƒo kple Abraham nu la, edzo; eye Abraham hã trɔ yi aƒe.

ref: That evening, while Lot was sitting near the city gate, the two angels arrived in Sodom. When Lot saw them, he got up, bowed down low,
text: Mawudɔla eveawo va ɖo Sodom le fiẽ me, eye Lot bɔbɔ nɔ Sodom gbɔ nu. Ke esi Lot kpɔ wo la, etsi tre yi ɖakpe wo, eye wobɔbɔ tsyɔ mo anyi.

ref: and said, “Gentlemen, I am your servant. Please come to my home. You can wash your feet, spend the night, and be on your way in the morning.” They told him, “No, we'll spend the night in the city square.”
text: Eye wògblɔ bena: Oo, nye aƒetɔwo, mitrɔ va dze miaƒe dɔla gbɔ, ne míatsi adɔ, eye miaklɔ miaƒe afɔwo ŋu; etsɔ ŋdi kanya hafi miagadze miaƒe mɔ dzi. Ke woɖo ŋu ne bena: O, míatsi dugbɔdzi le ablɔ me adɔ boŋ.

ref: But Lot kept insisting, until they finally agreed and went home with him. He quickly baked some bread, cooked a meal, and they ate.
text: Eye esi wòƒoe ɖe wo nu ʋuu la, wòtrɔ va dze eƒe me. Eye wòto tatali heɖo kplɔ̃ na wo, eye woɖu nu.

ref: Before Lot and his guests could go to bed, every man in Sodom, young and old, came and stood outside his house
text: Ke esi womekpɔ mia anyi haɖe o la, Sodom ŋutsuwo, ɖekakpuiwo kple ame tsitsiwo, keke dua ƒe mlɔenutɔwo ke hã va, eye woɖe to ɖe aƒe la.

ref: and started shouting, “Where are your visitors? Send them out, so we can have sex with them!”
text: Tete wodo ɣli yɔ Lot gblɔ bena: Afika ŋutsu, siwo va gbɔwò egbe fiẽ la le? Kplɔ wo do go va mía gbɔe, ne míanya wo.

ref: Lot went outside and shut the door behind him.
text: Eye Lot do go yi wo gbɔ le agbo la godo, eye wòtu ʋɔ ɖewo nu.

ref: Then he said, “Friends, please don't do such a terrible thing!
text: Eye wògblɔ bena: Nɔvinyewo, migawɔ nu baɖa o!

ref: I have two daughters who have never had sex. I'll bring them out, and you can do what you want with them. But don't harm these men. They are guests in my home.”
text: Míkpɔ ɖa, vinyenyɔnu eve li, amesiwo medo ŋutsu gbɔ kpɔ o, makplɔ wo vɛ na mi, ne miawɔ nusi mielɔ̃ la, ɖe wo ŋu faa; ɖeko migawɔ nu baɖa ɖe ŋutsu siawo ŋu o, elabena esiata wova dze gbɔnye ɖo.

ref: “Don't get in our way,” the crowd answered. “You're a foreigner. What right do you have to order us around? We'll do worse things to you than we're going to do to them.” The crowd kept arguing with Lot. Finally, they rushed toward the door to break it down.
text: Eye wodo ɣli ɖe eta gblɔ bena: Te ɖa afia! Wò amedzro ɖeka hã sia, si va dze mía gbã la, nanɔ ɖokuiwò wum amegãe alea? Azɔ la miawo vo ɖe wòa ŋuwò wu woawo gɔ̃. Eye wonyẽ zi ɖe Lot dzi gbidigbidi, be yewoagbã ʋɔa.

ref: But the two angels in the house reached out and pulled Lot safely inside.
text: Ke ŋutsuawo do abɔ ɖa he Lot va wo ɖokui gbee le aƒea me, eye wotu ʋɔa.

ref: Then they struck blind everyone in the crowd, and none of them could even find the door.
text: Eye wotsɔ ŋkuagbã ƒo ŋutsu, siwo le xexe le ʋɔtrua nu, ɖeviwo kple ame tsitsiwo siaa, ale bena ɖeɖi koe te wo ŋu dzodzro le ʋɔtrua nu didi me. Lot ɖeɖe.

ref: Lot went to the men who were engaged to his daughters and said, “Hurry up and get out of here! The Lord is going to destroy this city.” But they thought he was joking, and they laughed at him.
text: Tete Lot do go yi ɖagblɔe na toa, siwo le vianyɔnuwo ɖe ge la bena: Mitso, ne míado le ɖu la me, elabena Yehowa le etsrɔ̃ ge! Ke toawo bui nukonyae.

ref: Early the next morning the two angels tried to make Lot hurry and leave. They said, “Take your wife and your two daughters and get away from here as fast as you can! If you don't, every one of you will be killed when the Lord destroys the city.”
text: Esi dzite tso la, mawudɔlawo ƒo du ɖe Lot te gblɔ bena: Tso nakplɔ srɔ̃wò kple viwònyɔnu eve, siwo le gbɔwò la, ne màgatsrɔ̃ le ɖu la ƒe vodada ta o!

ref: At first, Lot just stood there. But the Lord wanted to save him. So the angels took Lot, his wife, and his two daughters by the hand and led them out of the city.
text: Ke esi wòle tɔtrɔm la, ŋutsuawo lé eya kple srɔ̃a kpakple vianyɔnu eveawo ƒe alɔnu hekplɔ wo do goe, eye woɖe asi le wo ŋu ɖe du la godo; elabena Yehowa ve woawo nu.

ref: When they were outside, one of the angels said, “Run for your lives! Don't even look back. And don't stop in the valley. Run to the hills, where you'll be safe.”
text: Eye esi wokplɔ wo do goe vo la, wogblɔ bena: Ekpo ɖe asiwò azɔ, eyata ɖe ɖokuiwò! Megakpɔ megbe o, eye megatɔ ɖe afia ɖeke hã o; ke boŋ si yi ɖe to la dzi, bena màgatsrɔ̃ o!

ref: Lot answered, “You have done us a great favor, sir. You have saved our lives, but please don't make us go to the hills. That's too far away. The city will be destroyed before we can get there, and we will be killed when it happens.
text: Kpɔ ɖa, ève wò ɖɔla nu xoxo, eye wò amenuveve, si nètsɔ ve nunye be, megale agbe la, esɔ gbã. Gake ɖewo nyemate ŋu asi ade toa dzi, hafi gbegblẽ la nava tum, eye maku o mahi.

ref: There's a town near here. It's only a small place, but my family and I will be safe, if you let us go there.”
text: Kpɔ ɖa, dua ɖe nye kem tsɔ ɖe mía gbɔ, ne masi ayi ɖe afima. Du suea ɖe ko wònye; eyata na mɔm, nemasi ayi ɖe eme, bena matsi agbe! Alo menye du suea ɖe ko wònye oa?

ref: “All right, go there,” he answered. “I won't destroy that town.
text: Eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Le nya sia me hã meɖo to wò, eye nyemagblẽ du, si nẽwɔnɛ la o.

ref: Hurry! Run! I can't do anything until you are safely there.” The town was later called Zoar because Lot had said it was small. Sodom and Gomorrah Are Destroyed
text: Ɖe abla yi afima kaba; elabena nyemate ŋu awɔ naneke o, vaseɖe esime naɖo afima. Eyata woyɔa ɖu la be Zoar.

ref: The sun was coming up as Lot reached the town of Zoar,
text: Eye ɣe dze, hafi Lot de Zoar. Sodom tsɔtsrɔ̃.

ref: and the Lord sent burning sulfur down like rain on Sodom and Gomorrah.
text: Eye Yehowa na aŋɔ kple dzo dza tso dziƒo tso Yehowa gbɔ ɖe Sodom kple Gomora dzi.

ref: He destroyed those cities and everyone who lived in them, as well as their land and the trees and grass that grew there.
text: Eye wotsrɔ̃ du siawo kple nuto ma me katã; dua me nɔlawo tsrɔ̃, eye nusiwo miena ɖe anyigba dzi hã gblẽ.

ref: On the way, Lot's wife looked back and was turned into a block of salt.
text: Ke srɔ̃a nye kɔ kpɔ megbe, eye wòzu dzekɔ.

ref: That same morning Abraham got up and went to the place where he had stood and spoken with the Lord.
text: Eye Abraham fɔ ŋdi kanya yi ɖe teƒe, si wòtsi tre ɖe Yehowa ƒe ŋku me le tsã la.

ref: He looked down toward Sodom and Gomorrah and saw smoke rising from all over the land—it was like a flaming furnace.
text: Eye esi wòkpɔ Sodom kple Gomora kple afima nutowo katã me le abu me ɖa la, kpo ɖa, dzudzɔ gãa ɖe le dzi dem tso anyigba la dzi abe aŋɔɖaƒe ene.

ref: When God destroyed the cities of the valley where Lot lived, he remembered his promise to Abraham and saved Lot from the terrible destruction. Moab and Ammon
text: Esi Mawu tsrɔ̃ nuto ma me duawo la, eɖo ŋku Abraham dzi, eye wòɖe Lot hekplɔe tso ɖu, siwo me Lot nɔ la ƒe gbegblẽ me. Lot vinyɔnuwo wɔ nuvɔ̃.
