ref: Lot was afraid to stay on in Zoar. So he took his two daughters and moved to a cave in the hill country.
text: Eye Lot kple vianyɔnu eveawo dzo le Zoar yi ɖanɔ to la dzi, elabena ele Zoar nɔnɔ vɔ̃m, eye woa kple vianyɔnu eveawo va no agadoa ɖe me.

ref: One day his older daughter said to her sister, “Our father is old, and there are no men anywhere for us to marry.
text: Eye tsitsitɔ gblɔ na ɖevitɔ bena: Mía fofo tsi, eye ŋutsua ɖeke megale anyigba dzi aɖe mí le amegbetɔwo ƒe kɔ la nu o.

ref: Let's get our father drunk! Then we can sleep with him and have children.”
text: Va míana wein mia fofo wòano, eye míadɔ egbɔ, ne wòado fu mi míadzi vi.

ref: That night they got their father drunk, and the older daughter got in bed with him, but he was too drunk even to know she was there.
text: Tete wona wein wo fofo le zã ma me wono, eye tsitsitɔ yi ɖamlɔ fofoa gbɔ; ke eya menya naneke le eƒe egbɔ mɔmlɔ kple fɔfɔdzo ŋuti o.

ref: The next day the older daughter said to her sister, “I slept with my father last night. We'll get him drunk again tonight, so you can sleep with him, and we can each have a child.”
text: Esi ŋu ke la, tsitsitɔ la gblɔ na ɖevitɔ bena: Eto ƒe zã la memlɔ fofonye gbɔ. Míagana wòano wein egbe ƒe zã hã; ekema wò hã nayi aɖamlɔ egbɔ, bena mía fofo nado fu mí míadzi vi.

ref: That night they got their father drunk, and this time the younger sister slept with him. But once again he was too drunk even to know she was there.
text: Eye le zã sia me hã wona wein wo fofo wono, eye ɖevitɔ hã yi ɖamlɔ egbɔ; ke eya menya naneke le eƒe egbɔ mɔmlɔ kple fɔfɔdzo ŋuti o.

ref: That's how Lot's two daughters had children.
text: Ale wɔ Lot ɖo fu vianyɔnu eveawo.

ref: The older daughter named her son Moab, and he is the ancestor of the Moabites.
text: Eye tsitsiwo dzi ŋutsuvi, eye wòtsɔ ŋkɔ nɛ bena Moab; eya nye egbegbe Moabitɔwo tɔgbui.

ref: The younger daughter named her son Benammi, and he is the ancestor of the Ammonites.
text: Eye ɖevitɔ hã dzi ŋutsuvi, eye wòtsɔ ŋkɔ ne be Ben-Ami, eya nye Amonitɔwo tɔgbui.

ref: he told everyone that his wife Sarah was his sister. So King Abimelech of Gerar had Sarah brought to him.
text: Eye Abraham gblɔ le srɔ̃a Sara ŋuti bena, ye nɔvinyɔnue; eyata Abimelex, Gerar-fia, na wokplɔ Sara vɛ na ye.

ref: But God came to Abimelech in a dream and said, “You have taken a married woman into your home, and for this you will die!”
text: Ke le zã me la Mawu va Abimelex gbɔ le ɖrɔ̃eƒe gblɔ nɛ bena: Kpɔ ɖa, àku kokoko le nyɔnu, si nèxɔ la ta, elabena ame srɔ̃e.

ref: Abimelech said to the Lord, “Don't kill me! I haven't slept with Sarah. Didn't they say they were brother and sister? I am completely innocent.”
text: Alo menye eya ŋutɔ gblɔ nam bena, ye nɔvinyɔnue, eye nyɔnua hã gblɔ nam bena, ye nɔviŋutsue oa? Nye dzime kɔ, eye nye asi dza le ekpɔkplɔme.

ref: Then God continued: I know you are innocent. That's why I kept you from sleeping with Sarah and doing anything wrong.
text: Tete Mawu gblɔ nɛ le drɔ̃eƒe bena: Nye hã menyae bena, wò dzime kɔ le ewɔwɔ me; eyata metso wò xɔ ɖo, be nagade asi eŋu ahawɔ nuvɔ̃ ɖe ŋunye o.

ref: Her husband is a prophet. Let her go back to him, and his prayers will save you from death. But if you don't return her, you and all your people will die.
text: Azɔ gagbugbɔ nyɔnu la na srɔ̃a, elabena nyagblɔɖila wònye, eye wòaɖe kuku ɖe tawo, be natsi agbe. Ke ne meɖe asi le eŋu o la, ekema nyae bena, mia kple aƒewòmetɔwo katã miaku.

ref: Early the next morning Abimelech sent for his officials, and when he told them what had happened, they were frightened.
text: Esi Abimelex fɔ ŋdi la, eya eƒe dɔlawo katã vɛ heto nya siawo katã na wo, eye ŋutsuawo vɔ ŋutɔ.

ref: Abimelech then called in Abraham and said: Look what you've done to us! What have I ever done to you? Why did you make me and my nation guilty of such a terrible sin?
text: Tete Abimelex yɔ Abraham, eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Nuka nye esi nèwɔ ɖe mía ŋu? Nuvɔ̃ ka gɔ̃ mewɔ de ŋuwò bena, nèhe dzɔgbevɔ̃e gã sia va nye ŋutɔ kple nye fiaɖuƒe la dzi mahã? Nusi womewɔna o la, eya nye esia nèwɔ ɖe ŋutinye.

ref: What were you thinking when you did this?
text: Eye Abimelex gagblɔ na Abraham bena: Nukae nèɖo ɖe ŋunye, hafi wɔ nu sia?

ref: Abraham answered: I did it because I didn't think any of you respected God, and I was sure that someone would kill me to get my wife.
text: Abraham gblɔ bena: Mebu be, afisiatɔwo manya vɔ̃a Mawu o, eye woawum le srɔ̃nye ta mahi.

ref: Besides, she is my half sister. We have the same father, but different mothers.
text: Evɔ, nyateƒe, nɔvinyenyɔnue; elabena fofonye vie, gake menye danye vie o, eye meɖee.

ref: When God made us leave my father's home and start wandering, I told her, “If you really love me, then tell everyone that I am your brother.”
text: Esi Mawu gblɔ nam bena, maʋu le fofonye ƒe me ayi dzronyigba dzi la, eya me kee megblɔ nɛ bena: Ve nunye, eye afisiafi, si míava la, nagblɔ bena, ye nɔvi ŋutsu menye. Abimelex na wogakplɔ Sara vɛ na Abraham kple nunanawo.

ref: After Abimelech had given Abraham some sheep, cattle, and slaves, he sent Sarah back
text: Tete Abimelex tso alẽwo, nyiwo, kluviwo kple kosiviwo na Abraham, eye wògagbugbɔ srɔ̃a Sara hã nɛ.

ref: and told Abraham he could settle anywhere in his country.
text: Eye Abimelex gblɔ bena: Nye anyigba enye si, no afisiafi, si nyo ŋuwò.

ref: Meanwhile, God had kept Abimelech's wife and slaves from having children. But Abraham prayed, and God let them start having children again.
text: Elabena Yehowa xe nyɔnu, siwo katã le Abimelex ƒe me la ƒe vidzidɔ le Sara, Abraham srɔ̃ la ta.

ref: The Lord was good to Sarah and kept his promise.
text: Ke Yehowa ɖo ŋku ŋugbe, si wòɖo na Sara la dzi, eye Yehowa wɔ na Sara, abe alesi wògblɔ ene.

ref: Although Abraham was very old, Sarah had a son exactly at the time God had said.
text: Ale Sara fɔ fu, eye wòdzi ŋutsuvi na Abraham le eƒe amegãkuku me le ɣeyiɣi, si Mawu gblɔ ɖi xoxo la dzi.

ref: and when the boy was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him, just as God had commanded.
text: Esi via Isak xɔ ŋkeke enyi la, Abraham tso aʋa ne, abe alesi Yehowa de se nɛ ene.

ref: and Sarah said, “God has made me laugh. And now everyone will laugh with me.
text: Eye Sara gblɔ bena: Mawu do nukokoe nam, eye amesiame, si asee la, ako nu le ŋutinye.

ref: Who would have dared to tell Abraham that someday I would have a child? But in his old age, I have given him a son.”
text: Eye wògblɔ bena: Ameka susu le Abraham ŋuti bena, Sara ana no viwò mahã? Elabena medzi ŋutsuvi nɛ le eƒe amegãkuku me.

ref: The time came when Sarah no longer had to nurse Isaac, and on that day Abraham gave a big feast. Hagar and Ishmael Are Sent Away
text: Eye ɖevi la tsi, eye wòɖi no, eye gbesigbe Isak ɖi no la, Abraham ɖo kplɔ̃ gãa ɖe. Ismael kple dada nyanyã.

ref: One day, Sarah noticed Hagar's son Ishmael playing, and she said to Abraham, “Get rid of that Egyptian slave woman and her son! I don't want him to inherit anything. It should all go to my son.”
text: egblɔ na Abraham bena: Nyã kosi sia kple via ɖa; elabena kosi sia ƒe vi mate ŋu anyi dome kple vinye Isak o.

ref: But God said, “Abraham, don't worry about your slave woman and the boy. Just do what Sarah tells you. Isaac will inherit your family name,
text: Eye Mawu gblɔ na Abraham bena: Megana nya sia nave wò le ŋutsuvi la kple wò kosi la ta o; ke boŋ ɖo to Sara le nusianu, si wodi tso gbɔwò la me, elabena Isak me wò dzidzime-viwo adzo tso.

ref: but the son of the slave woman is also your son, and I will make his descendants into a great nation.”
text: Ke mana dukɔ nadzo tso kosi la ƒe vi hã me, elabena viwoe.

ref: Early the next morning Abraham gave Hagar an animal skin full of water and some bread. Then he put the boy on her shoulder and sent them away. They wandered around in the desert near Beersheba,
text: Eye Abraham fɔ ŋdi kanya tso abolo, eye woku tsi ɖe lãgbalẽgolo me taŋ, eye wotsɔ ɖevi la kple nu mawo ɖo abɔta na Hagar hedo mɔ wo. Ke esi wole yiyim la, wova tra mɔ le Beer-Seba gbe dzi.

ref: and after they had run out of water, Hagar put her son under a bush.
text: Esi lãgbalẽgolo me tsi la vɔ la, etsɔ ɖevi la yi ɖamlɔ ati dodoea ɖe te,

ref: When God heard the boy crying, the angel of God called out to Hagar from heaven and said, “Hagar, why are you worried? Don't be afraid. I have heard your son crying.
text: Ke Mawu se ɖevia ƒe avifafa, eye mawudɔla yɔ Hagar tso dziƒo gblɔ nɛ bena: Hagar, nuka le wɔwòm? Megavɔ̃ o, elabena Mawu se ɖevi la ƒe gbe tso afisi wòle.

ref: Help him up and hold his hand, because I will make him the father of a great nation.”
text: Tsi tre, yi ɖakɔ ɖevi la, eye nalée ɖe asi, elabena mana woazu dukɔ gãa ɖe.

ref: Then God let her see a well. So she went to the well and filled the skin with water, then gave some to her son.
text: Tete Mawu ʋu eƒe ŋkuwo, eye wòkpɔ tsivudoa ɖe. Enumake woyi ɖaku tsi ɖe lãgbalẽgolo la me taŋ, eye wòna ɖevia wono.

ref: About this time Abimelech and his army commander Phicol said to Abraham, “God blesses everything you do!
text: Le ɣemaɣi me la Abimelex kple eƒe aʋafia Pixol wogblɔ na Abraham bena: Mawu li kpli wò le nusianu, si nèwɔna la me.

ref: Now I want you to promise in the name of God that you will always be loyal to me and my descendants, just as I have always been loyal to you in this land where you have lived as a foreigner.”
text: Azɔ ka atam le Mawu ƒe ŋku me le afisia be, nagable nye ŋutɔ, vinyewo kple nye dzidzimeviwo o! Abe alesi meve nu wòe ene la, nenema ke wò hã nawɔ nam kple anyigba, si dzi nènye amedzro le la!

ref: And so, Abraham promised he would.
text: Eye Abraham gblɔ bena: Melɔ̃, maka atam la.

ref: One day, Abraham told Abimelech, “Some of your servants have taken over one of my wells.”
text: Tete Abraham ƒo nu kple Abimelex le tsivudo, si eƒe dɔlawo xɔ sesẽe la ŋuti.

ref: “This is the first I've heard about it,” Abimelech replied. “Why haven't you said something before? I don't have any idea who did it.”
text: Abimelex xɔ edzi ne bena: Nyemenya amesi wɔ nu sia o, eye wò hã mègblɔ nyaa ɖeke le eŋuti nam kpɔ o, egbe ko hafi nye hã mese nyaa ɖe le eŋu.

ref: Abraham gave Abimelech some sheep and cattle, then the two men made a peace treaty.
text: Tete Abraham tsɔ alẽwo kple nyiwo na Abimelex, eye wo kple eve wòbia nu.

ref: Abraham separated seven female lambs from his flock of sheep,
text: Ke Abraham ɖe alẽvi adre ɖo vovo.

ref: and Abimelech asked, “Why have you done this?”
text: Eye Abimelex bia Abraham be: Alẽvi adre, siwo neɖo vovo la gome ɖe?

ref: Abraham replied, “I want you to accept these seven lambs as proof that I dug this well.”
text: Eɖo ŋu nɛ bena: Alẽvi adre siawo naxa le asinye, ne wòanye ɖase nam bena, nyee ɖe vudo sia.

ref: So they called the place Beersheba, because they made a treaty there.
text: Eyata woyɔ teƒe ma bena: Beer-Seba, elabena afima wo ame eve la woka atam na wo nɔewo le.

ref: When the treaty was completed, Abimelech and his army commander Phicol went back to the land of the Philistines.
text: Eye esi wòbia nu le Beer-Seba la, Abimelex kple eƒe aʋafia Pixol wodze mɔ gatrɔ yi ɖe Filistitowo ƒe anyigba dzi.

ref: Abraham planted a tamarisk tree in Beersheba and worshiped the eternal Lord God.
text: Eye Abraham do atiwo ɖe Beer-Seba, eye wòyɔ Yehowa Mawu, si le agbe tegbee la ƒe ŋkɔ dzi le afima.

ref: Then Abraham lived a long time as a foreigner in the land of the Philistines.
text: Ke Abraham nye amedzro le Filistitowo ƒe anyigba dzi eteƒe didi.

ref: Some years later God decided to test Abraham, so he spoke to him. Abraham answered, “Here I am, Lord.”
text: Le nya siawo megbe la Mawu do Abraham kpɔ, eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Abraham! Eɖo ŋu nɛ bena: Nye enye si!

ref: The Lord said, “Go get Isaac, your only son, the one you dearly love! Take him to the land of Moriah, and I will show you a mountain where you must sacrifice him to me on the fires of an altar.”
text: Eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Kplɔ Isak, wò tenuvi, si gbɔ mèlɔ̃a nu lena o la, yi ɖe Moria-nyigba dzi, eye natsɔe amè nui le afima le vɔsamlekpui dzi le toa ɖe, si mafia wò la dzi!

ref: So Abraham got up early the next morning and chopped wood for the fire. He put a saddle on his donkey and set out with Isaac and two servants for the place where God had told him to go.
text: Tete Abraham tɔ ŋdi kanya, bia akpa na eƒe tedzi, eye wòkplɔ dɔla eve kple via Isak ɖe asi, eye esi wòdze nake ɖe numemea ŋu la, edze mɔ yi teƒe, si Mawu gblɔ nɛ la.

ref: Three days later Abraham looked off in the distance and saw the place.
text: Le ŋkeke etɔ̃agbe la Abraham wu mo dzi kpɔ teƒe la le adzɔge ɖa ʋĩ.

ref: He told his servants, “Stay here with the donkey, while my son and I go over there to worship. We will come back.”
text: Tete Abraham gblɔ na dɔlawo bena: Mitsi afisia kple tedzi la; ke mía kple vinye la míayi afime aɖado gbe ɖa, emegbe míagatrɔ ava mia gbɔ.

ref: Abraham put the wood on Isaac's shoulder, but he carried the hot coals and the knife. As the two of them walked along,
text: Eye Abraham tsɔ numènake la ɖo la na via Isak, ke eya ŋutɔ ɖe dzo, eye wòɖo hɛ asi; ale wo kpakple eve woyi.

ref: Isaac said, “Father, we have the coals and the wood, but where is the lamb for the sacrifice?” “My son,” Abraham answered, “God will provide the lamb.” The two of them walked on, and
text: Abraham xɔ edzi bena: Vinye, Mawu ŋutɔ aɖi alẽ miatsɔ amè nui. Eye wo kpakple eve wogayi ŋgɔ.

ref: when they reached the place that God had told him about, Abraham built an altar and placed the wood on it. Next, he tied up his son and put him on the wood.
text: Esi woɖo teƒe, si Mawu gblɔ nɛ la, Abraham ɖi vɔsamlekpui, eye wòtɔ nakeawo ɖo edzi; emegbe la ebla via Isak, eye wòkɔe mlɔ nake, siwo le vɔsamlekpuia dzi la dzi.

ref: He then took the knife and got ready to kill his son.
text: Eye Abraham do asi ɖa tsɔ hɛ, be yeawu via.

ref: But the Lord's angel shouted from heaven, “Abraham! Abraham!” “Here I am!” he answered.
text: Tete Yehowa ƒe dɔla yɔe tso dziƒo gblɔ bena: Abraham, Abraham! Eye wòtɔ bena: Nye enye si!

ref: “Don't hurt the boy or harm him in any way!” the angel said. “Now I know that you truly obey God, because you were willing to offer him your only son.”
text: Eye wògblɔ bena: Megaka asi ɖevi la ŋu o, eye megawɔ nanekee o! Menyae azɔ bena, èvɔ̃a Mawu, elabena mete wò tenuvi lam o.

ref: Abraham looked up and saw a ram caught by its horns in the bushes. So he took the ram and sacrificed it instead of his son.
text: Tete Abraham fɔ kɔ dzi, eye kpɔ ɖa, agboa ɖe nye ekem, si ƒe dzowo ku aveka le emegbe, eye Abraham yi ɖalé agbo la hetsɔ mè nui ɖe via teƒe.

ref: Abraham named that place “The Lord Will Provide.” And even now people say, “On the mountain of the Lord it will be provided.”
text: Eye Abraham yɔ teƒe ma bena: Afisi Yehowa ɖe eɖokui fia le. Eyata wogblɔna vaseɖe egbegbe bena: Le to dzi, afisi Yehowa ɖea eɖokui fiana le. Mawu ɖo kpe ŋugbedodo la dzi.

ref: The Lord's angel called out from heaven a second time:
text: Eye Yehowa ƒe dɔla gayɔ Abraham zi evelia tso dziƒo

ref: You were willing to offer your only son to the Lord, and so he makes you this solemn promise,
text: hegblɔ bena: Mefa ɖokuinye, elabena esi nèwɔ nu sia, eye mete wò tenuvi ɖeka lam o la,

ref: “I will bless you and give you such a large family, that someday your descendants will be more numerous than the stars in the sky or the grains of sand along the seashore. They will defeat their enemies and take over the cities where their enemies live.
text: eyata mayra wò geɖe ŋutɔ, eye viwòwo asɔ gbɔ fũ abe dziƒoɣletiviwo kple ƒutake ene, eye wò dzidzimeviwo atsɔ woƒe futɔwo ƒe duwo anyi domee.

ref: You have obeyed me, and so you and your descendants will be a blessing to all nations on earth.”
text: To wò dzidzimeviwo me woayra anyigba dzi dukɔwo katã le, elabena èɖo to nye gbe.

ref: Abraham and Isaac went back to the servants who had come with him, and they returned to Abraham's home in Beersheba. The Children of Nahor
text: Emegbe Abraham gatrɔ va eƒe dɔlawo gbɔ, eye wodze mɔ yi Beer-Seba, eye Abraham nɔ Beer-Seba. Nahor ƒe dzidzimeviwo.

ref: Nahor also had another wife. Her name was Reumah, and she had four sons: Tebah, Gaham, Tahash, and Maacah.
text: Eye srɔ̃ evelia ŋkɔ nye Rehuma, eya hã dzi Tebax, Gaham, Tahas kple Maxa nɛ.

ref: he went to the Hittites and said,
text: Eye Abraham tso le ame kuku la gbɔ, eye wòƒo nu kple Xitiviwo bena:

ref: “I live as a foreigner in your land, and I don't own any property where I can bury my wife. Please let me buy a piece of land.”
text: Amedzro menye le mia dome, mina ameɖiƒem, bena matsɔ nye ame kuku la aɖi ɖe afima!

ref: “Sir,” they answered, “you are an important man. Choose the best place to bury your wife. None of us would refuse you a resting place for your dead.”
text: Amegã, se mía gbe! Mawu ƒe ame ɖoɖoa nenye le mia dome; ɖi wò ame kuku la ɖe míaƒe ame veviwo ɖiƒe. Mía dometɔa ɖeke magbe bena, megaɖi wò ame kukua ɖe eƒe ameɖiƒe o.

ref: and replied, “If you are willing to let me bury my wife here, please ask Zohar's son Ephron
text: eye wòƒo nu kpli wo bena: Ne mielɔ̃ bena, maɖi nye ame kukua la, ekema miɖo tom, eye miɖe kuku na Efron Zoxar-vi nam

ref: to sell me Machpelah Cave at the end of his field. I'll pay what it's worth, and all of you can be witnesses.”
text: be, wòatsɔ eƒe kpeto Maxpela, si le eƒe agble ƒe nuwuƒe la nam, eye maxe fe ɖe eta blibo, ne wòanye nye ameɖiƒe koŋ le mia dome.

ref: Ephron was sitting there near the city gate, when Abraham made this request, and he answered,
text: Ke Efron le Xitiviawo dome Eye Efron Xititɔ la ɖo ŋu na Abraham le Xitiviwo kple amesiwo katã va dua me la ƒe ŋku me bena:

ref: “Sir, the whole field, including the cave, is yours. With my own people as witnesses, I freely give it to you as a burial place for your dead.”
text: Nye amegã, ɖo tom! Metsɔ agble lã kple kpeto, si le eme la, ke wò. Le nye dumetɔwo katã ŋku me metsɔe ke wò, ɖi wò ame kuku la ko!

ref: Once again, Abraham bowed down
text: Tete Abraham bɔbɔ na duametɔwo,

ref: and said to Ephron, “In front of these witnesses, I offer you the full price, so I can bury my wife. Please accept my offer.”
text: eye wòɖo ŋu na Efron bena: Mi duameviwo misee! Maxe fe ɖe agble la ta; meɖe kuku na wò, xɔ ga le asinye, be mate ŋu aɖi nye ame kukua ɖe afima!

ref: Abraham accepted Ephron's offer and paid him the
text: zu Abraham tɔ le Xitiviawo kple amesiwo katã va dua me la ŋku me.

ref: So Abraham buried his wife Sarah in Machpelah Cave that was in the field
text: Esia megbe la Abraham ɖi srɔ̃a Sara ɖe kpeto, si le Maxpela-gblea me la me le Mamre kasa; esia enye Hebron le Kanaan-nyigba dzi.

ref: Abraham was now a very old man. The Lord had made him rich, and he was successful in everything he did.
text: Eye Abraham tsi xɔ ƒe geɖe, eye Yehowa yra Abraham le nuwo katã me.

ref: One day, Abraham called in his most trusted servant and said to him, “Solemnly promise me
text: Eye Abraham gblɔ na eƒe dɔla tsitsitɔ, si kpɔa eƒe nuwo katã dzi bena. Tsɔ wò asi ɖo nye ali me,

ref: in the name of the Lord, who rules heaven and earth, that you won't choose a wife for my son Isaac from the people here in the land of Canaan.
text: eye nata Yehowa, dziƒo kple anyigba ƒe Mawu la nam be, maɖe srɔ̃ na vinye atso Kanaantɔ, siwo dome mele la ƒe nyɔnuviwo dome o;

ref: Instead, go back to the land where I was born and find a wife for him from among my relatives.”
text: ke boŋ nayi mía denyigba dzi kple ƒonyemeawo gbɔ, eye naɖe srɔ̃ na vinye Isak!

ref: But the servant asked, “What if the young woman I choose refuses to leave home and come here with me? Should I send Isaac there to look for a wife?”
text: Dɔla la ɖo ŋu ne bena: Evɔ, ne nyɔnua mehelɔ̃ be, yeakplɔm ɖo ava anyigba sia dzi o la, ɖe magbugbɔ viwò la ayi ɖe anyigba, si dzi neʋu tso la dzii mahã?

ref: “No!” Abraham answered. “Don't ever do that, no matter what.
text: Abraham gblɔ nɛ bena: Kpɔ nyuie, bena nagagbugbɔ vinye la ayi afimae o!

ref: The Lord who rules heaven brought me here from the land where I was born and promised that he would give this land to my descendants forever. When you go back there, the Lord will send his angel ahead of you to help you find a wife for my son.
text: Yehowa, dziƒo Mawu, si ɖem tso fofonye ƒe me kple mia denyigba dzi, amesi ƒo nu kplim, eye wòka atam nam bena: Wò dzidzimeviwo matsɔ anyigba sia ana la, adɔ eƒe ɖɔla ɖe ŋgɔwò, be naɖe srɔ̃ na vinye la tso afima

ref: If the woman refuses to come along, you don't have to keep this promise. But don't ever take my son back there.”
text: Eye ne nyɔnua melɔ̃ be, yeakpɔ wò ɖo o la, ekema menye wòe tu nye atam sia o; ɖeko megakpɔ vinye la yi afimae gbeɖe o!

ref: So the servant gave Abraham his word that he would do everything he had been told to do.
text: Eye dɔla la tsɔ eƒe asi ɖo eƒe aƒetɔ Abraham ƒe ali me, eye wòka atam nɛ ɖe nya sia dzi.

ref: Soon after that, the servant loaded ten of Abraham's camels with valuable gifts. Then he set out for the city in northern Syria, where Abraham's brother Nahor lived.
text: Tete dɔla la ɖe kposɔ ewo tso eƒe aƒetɔ ƒe kposɔwo dome, eye wotsɔ eƒe aƒetɔ ƒe nu vevi vovovowo ɖe asi, eye wòdze mɔ heyi Mesopotamia, ɖe Naxor ƒe ɖu la me.

ref: When he got there, he let the camels rest near the well outside the city. It was late afternoon, the time when the women came out for water.
text: Le fiẽ, le nyɔnuwo ƒe tɔmedeɣi la, ena kposɔawo tsyɔ akɔ anyi ɖe dua xa le ʋudoa ɖe to.

ref: The servant prayed: You, Lord, are the God my master Abraham worships. Please keep your promise to him and let me find a wife for Isaac today.
text: Eye wòdo gbe ɖa gblɔ bena: Yehowa, ye aƒetɔ Abraham ƒe Mawu! Meɖe kuku, na wòadze edzi nam egbea, eye nave nye aƒetɔ Abraham nu!

ref: The young women of the city will soon come to this well for water,
text: Kpɔ ɖa, mele tsitre ɖe vudoa to, eye ne dua me nyɔnuviwo va tsi du ge,

ref: and I'll ask one of them for a drink. If she gives me a drink and then offers to get some water for my camels, I'll know she is the one you have chosen and that you have kept your promise to my master.
text: eye ava me be, nyɔnuvia ɖe ava, amesi magblɔ na bena: Na tsim mano, eye woaɖo ŋu nam be: Noe, eye mana wò kposɔwo hã nano lã, ekema eyae nye nyɔnu, si nèɖo ɖi na wò ɖɔla Isak, eye eya me madze sii le be, eve nye aƒetɔ nu!

ref: While he was still praying, a beautiful unmarried young woman came by with a water jar on her shoulder. She was Rebekah, the daughter of Bethuel, the son of Abraham's brother Nahor and his wife Milcah. Rebekah walked past Abraham's servant, then went over to the well, and filled her water jar. When she started back,
text: Eye nyɔnuvi la dze tugbe ŋutɔ, ɖetugbi, si ŋutsua ɖeke mede asi eŋu kpɔ o la wònye, eye wòyi ɖe vudoa me ɖaku tsi ɖe eƒe ze la me, eye wògado go.

ref: Abraham's servant ran to her and said, “Please let me have a drink of water.”
text: Tete dɔla la ƒu du yi ɖakpee gblɔ bena: Meɖe kuku na wò, na tsi via ɖem mano!

ref: “I'll be glad to,” she answered. Then she quickly took the jar from her shoulder and held it while he drank.
text: Eye wògblɔ bena: Amegã, anoe. Enumake wòdro ze la ɖi, eye wònae wono.

ref: After he had finished, she said, “Now I'll give your camels all the water they want.” She quickly poured out water for them, and she kept going back for more, until his camels had drunk all they wanted.
text: Enumake wòkɔ tsia ɖe lãto la me, eye wògaƒu du yi vudoa me ɖadu tsi la, eye woɖu tsi na eƒe kposɔwo katã.

ref: Abraham's servant did not say a word, but he watched everything Rebekah did, because he wanted to know for certain if this was the woman the Lord had chosen.
text: Ke ŋutsu la zi kpi le ekpɔm dũ, ne yeadze sii be, ɖe Yehowa na yeƒe mɔzɔzɔ dze dzi loo, alo medze edzi o.

ref: The servant had brought along an expensive gold ring and two large gold bracelets. When Rebekah had finished bringing the water, he gave her the ring for her nose and the bracelets for her arms.
text: Esi kposɔwo ɖi kɔ la, ŋutsu la tsɔ sikaŋɔtigɛ, si da sekel afã, kple alɔnugɛ eve, siwo da sekel ewo la, do nɛ.

ref: Then he said, “Please tell me who your father is. Does he have room in his house for me and my men to spend the night?”
text: Eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Ameka vi nènyea? Meɖe kuku, gblɔe nam, ɖe dzeƒe le fofowò ƒe mea?

ref: She answered, “My father is Bethuel, the son of Nahor and Milcah.
text: Eɖo ŋu ne bena: Betuel, Milka vi, si wòdzi na Naxor la vinyɔnu menye.

ref: We have a place where you and your men can stay, and we also have enough straw and feed for your camels.”
text: Eye wògagblɔ nɛ bena: Aƒla kple gbe le mia si lu, eye dzeƒe hã li.

ref: Then the servant bowed his head and prayed,
text: Eye ŋutsu la dze klo na Yehowa hetsyɔ mo anyi

ref: “I thank you, Lord God of my master Abraham! You have led me to his relatives and kept your promise to him.”
text: gblɔ bena: Woakafu Yehowa, nye aƒetɔ Abraham ƒe Mawu, amesi meŋlɔ nye aƒetɔ be le enu veve kple nuteƒewɔwɔ nɛ me o la. Elabena Yehowa fia mɔm, eye wòkplɔm va nye aƒetɔ nɔviwo gbɔe.

ref: Rebekah ran straight home and told her family everything.
text: Ke nyɔnuvi la ƒu du yi aƒe me, be woaɖagblɔ nya siawo na dadaa.

ref: Then Laban said, “The Lord has brought you safely here. Come home with me. There's no need for you to keep on standing outside. I have a room ready for you in our house, and there's also a place for your camels.”
text: Tete wògblɔ bena: Va, wò amesi Yehowa yra! Nukaŋuti nètsi dugbɔ dzi? Medzra xɔ me ɖo, eye teƒe hã li na kposɔwo.

ref: Abraham's servant went home with Laban, where Laban's servants unloaded his camels and gave them straw and feed. Then they brought water into the house, so Abraham's servant and his men could wash their feet.
text: Eye esi wòkplɔ ŋutsua va aƒe me la, eɖe akpa na kposɔawo, eye wòtsɔ aƒla kple gbe ve na kposɔawo; emegbe la eku tsi vɛ na ŋutsua kple amesiwo katã kpe ɖe eŋuti la, bena woaklɔ woƒe afɔwo.

ref: After that, they brought in food. But the servant said, “Before I eat, I must tell you why I have come.” “Go ahead and tell us,” Laban answered.
text: Ke esi wotsɔ nuɖuɖu ɖo ekɔme la, egblɔ bena: Nyemaɖu nu haɖe o, negbe ɖeko magblɔ nya, si mehe vɛ la hafi! Woɖo ŋu be: Gblɔe faa!

ref: The servant explained: I am Abraham's servant.
text: Eye wògblɔ bena: Abraham ƒe dɔla menye.

ref: The Lord has been good to my master and has made him very rich. He has given him many sheep, goats, cattle, camels, and donkeys, as well as a lot of silver and gold, and many slaves.
text: Yehowa yra nye aƒetɔ wòkpɔ nu ŋutɔ, eye wòna alẽwo kple nyiwo, klosalo kple sika, kluviwo kple kosiviwo, kpo-sɔwo kple tedziwoe.

ref: Sarah, my master's wife, didn't have any children until she was very old. Then she had a son, and my master has given him everything.
text: Eye nye aƒetɔ srɔ̃, Sara, dzi ŋutsuvi nanye aƒetɔ, esime woku nyagã, eye nye aƒetɔ tsɔ eƒe kesinɔnuwo katã na via.

ref: I solemnly promised my master that I would do what he said. And he told me, “Don't choose a wife for my son from the women in this land of Canaan.
text: Eye nye aƒetɔ gblɔ nam, eye wòna meka atam ɖe dzi nɛ bena: Megaɖe srɔ̃ na vinye tso Kanaantɔ, siwo ƒe anyigba dzi mele la ƒe nyɔnuviwo dome o;

ref: Instead, go back to the land where I was born and find a wife for my son from among my relatives.”
text: ke boŋ yi ɖe fofonye ƒe me, ɖe ƒonyemeawo gbɔ, eye naɖe srɔ̃ na vinye le afima!

ref: I asked my master, “What if the young woman refuses to come with me?”
text: Eye megblɔ na nye aƒetɔ bena: Ne nyɔnu la melɔ̃ be, yeakplɔm ɖo o ɖe?

ref: My master answered, “I have always obeyed the Lord, and he will send his angel to help you find my son a wife from among my own relatives.
text: Eɖo ŋu nam bena: Yehowa, amesi ƒe ŋku me zɔm mele la, adɔ eƒe dɔla akpe ɖe ŋuwò, bena wò mɔzɔzɔ la nadze edzi na wò, eye naɖe srɔ̃ na vinye atso ƒonyemeawo gbɔ le fofonye ƒe me.

ref: But if they refuse to let her come back with you, then you are freed from your promise.”
text: Ne ava ƒonyemeawo gbɔ, eye womelɔ̃ be, woatsɔe na wò o la, ekema menye wòe tu nye atam la o.

ref: When I came to the well today, I silently prayed, “You, Lord, are the God my master Abraham worships, so please lead me to a wife for his son
text: Esi meva ɖo vudoa to egbea la, medo gbe ɖagblɔ bena: Yehowa, wò nye aƒetɔ Abraham ƒe Mawu! Ne ana nye azɔli, si dzi mele, nadze edzi nam la, — kpɔ ɖa, mele tsitre ɖe vudoa to —

ref: while I am here at the well. When a young woman comes out to get water, I'll ask her to give me a drink.
text: ekema na bena, nyɔnuvi, si ava tsi ɖu ge, eye magblɔ nɛ bena: Na tsi via ɖem mano,

ref: If she gives me a drink and offers to get some water for my camels, I'll know she is the one you have chosen.”
text: eye woaɖo ŋu nam be: Noe, eye mana wò kposɔwo hã la, eya nanye nyɔnu, si Yehowa ɖo ɖi na nye aƒetɔ ƒe vi la!

ref: Even before I had finished praying, Rebekah came by with a water jar on her shoulder. When she had filled the jar, I asked her for a drink.
text: Esi nyemekpɔ ɖe nu le nya la me o la, tete Rebeka lé ze ɖe ta vɛ, eye wòyi ɖe vudo la me ɖaku tsi. Eye megblɔ ne bena. Meɖe kuku, na tsim mano!

ref: She quickly lowered the jar from her shoulder and said, “Have a drink. Then I'll get water for your camels.” So I drank, and after that she got some water for my camels.
text: Enumake wòdro eƒe ze la ɖi gblɔ bena: Noe, eye mana wò kposɔwo hã. Esi menoe vɔ la, ena kposɔwo hã wono.

ref: I asked her who her father was, and she answered, “My father is Bethuel the son of Nahor and Milcah.” At once I put the ring in her nose and the bracelets on her arms.
text: Emegbe mebiae bena: Ameka vi nènyea? Eɖo ŋu bena: Betuel, amesi Milka dzi na Naxor ƒe vi menye, eye metsɔ ŋɔtigɛ kple alɔnugɛ ɖo ne.

ref: Then I bowed my head and gave thanks to the God my master Abraham worships. The Lord had led me straight to my master's relatives, and I had found a wife for his son.
text: Tete medze klo na Yehowa, eye metsyɔ mo anyi hekafu Yehowa, nye aƒetɔ Abraham ƒe Mawu, si kplɔm nyuie ale, be maɖe nye aƒetɔ nɔvi ƒe vinyɔnu na ye aƒetɔ ƒe vi la.

ref: Now please tell me if you are willing to do the right thing for my master. Will you treat him fairly, or do I have to look for another young woman?
text: Azɔ, ne miedi be, miave nye aƒetɔ nu, eye miaɖi anukware nɛ la, ekema migblɔe nam; ke ne mielɔ̃ o la, migblɔe nam, bena nenye ɖusi me alo mia mee hã, ne maɖo tae.

ref: Laban and Bethuel answered, “The Lord has done this. We have no choice in the matter.
text: Tete Laban kple Betuel wogblɔ bena: Nya sia tso Yehowa gbɔ, eyata míate ŋu agblɔ nya vɔ̃ alo nya nyui ɖe eŋu o.

ref: Take Rebekah with you; she can marry your master's son, just as the Lord has said.”
text: Kpo ɖa, Rebeka anye si, ele asiwò me, kplɔe yii, ne wòanye wò aƒetɔ vi la srɔ̃, abe alesi Yehowa gblɔe ene!

ref: Abraham's servant bowed down and thanked the Lord.
text: Ke esi Abraham ƒe dɔla se nya siawo la, etsyɔ mo anyi na Yehowa.

ref: Then he gave clothing, as well as silver and gold jewelry, to Rebekah. He also gave expensive gifts to her brother and her mother.
text: Eye dɔla la ɖe klosalo- kple sikanuwo kple avɔ veviwo na Rebeka, eye wotsɔ akpedanu xɔasiwo na nɔvia kple dadaa.

ref: Abraham's servant and the men with him ate and drank, then spent the night there. The next morning they got up, and the servant told Rebekah's mother and brother, “I would like to go back to my master now.”
text: Esia megbe la eya kple ŋutsu, siwo kpe ɖe eŋu la, woɖu nu, eye wono nu, eye wotsi dɔ. Ke esi wofɔ ŋdi la, egblɔ bena: Mido mɔm, ne mayi nye aƒetɔ gbɔ!

ref: “Let Rebekah stay with us for a week or ten days,” they answered. “Then she may go.”
text: Eye nɔvia kple dadaa gblɔ bena: Na nyɔnuvi la nanɔ mía gbɔ ʋee abe ŋkeke ewo ene, emegbe la ayi faa.

ref: But he said, “Don't make me stay any longer. The Lord has already helped me find a wife for my master's son. Now let us return.”
text: Eɖo ŋu na wo bena: Migasikam ɖi o! Yehowa na nye mɔzɔzɔa dze edzi nam; eyata mido mɔm, ne mayi nye aƒetɔ gbɔ.

ref: They answered, “Let's ask Rebekah what she wants to do.”
text: Eye wogblɔ bena: Míayɔ nyɔnuvi la vɛ, bena míabia eya ŋutɔ ase.

ref: They called her and asked, “Are you willing to leave with this man at once?” “Yes,” she answered.
text: Tete woyɔ Rebeka vɛ, eye wobiae bena: ayi kple ŋutsu sia? Eɖo eŋu bena: E, mayi.

ref: So they agreed to let Rebekah and an old family servant woman leave immediately with Abraham's servant and his men.
text: Eye wodo mɔ wo nɔvi Rebeka kple amesi na noe le ɖevime la, kpe ɖe Abraham ƒe dɔla kple eŋumewo ŋuti.

ref: They gave Rebekah their blessing and said, “We pray that God will give you many children and grandchildren and that he will help them defeat their enemies.”
text: Eye woyra Rebeka gblɔ nɛ bena: Wò, mía nɔvinyɔnu, nadzi ame akpe nanewo, eye viwòwo natsɔ woƒe futɔwo ƒe duwo anyi domee!

ref: Afterwards, Rebekah and the young women who were to travel with her prepared to leave. Then they got on camels and left with Abraham's servant and his men.
text: Esia megbe Rebeka kple eƒe nyɔnudɔlawo ɖe kposɔwo dzi, eye wodze mɔ dze ŋutsu la yome. Ale ŋutsu la kplɔ Rebeka hedzoe.

ref: At that time Isaac was living in the southern part of Canaan near a place called “The Well of the Living One Who Sees Me.”
text: Ke Isak gbɔ tso Agbetɔ, si le kpɔyem la ƒe vudo me; elabena enɔ dziehenyigba dzi.

ref: One evening he was walking out in the fields, when suddenly he saw a group of people approaching on camels. So he started toward them. Rebekah saw him coming; she got down from her camel, and asked, “Who is that man?” “He is my master Isaac,” the servant answered. Then Rebekah covered her face with her veil.
text: eye wòbia ɖɔla la bena: Ŋutsu ka enye kem le gbe me zɔm gbɔna mía kpe ge? Dɔla la ɖo ŋu nɛ bena: Nye aƒetɔe. Tete wòtsɔ motsyɔnu tsyɔ ŋku me.

ref: The servant told Isaac everything that had happened.
text: Esia megbe la dɔla la xlẽ nusiwo katã wòwɔ la fia Isak.

ref: Isaac took Rebekah into the tent where his mother had lived before she died, and Rebekah became his wife. He loved her and was comforted over the loss of his mother.
text: Eye Isak kplɔe yi ɖe dada Sara ƒe agbadɔ me, eye wòɖe Rebeka wòzu srɔ̃a, eye wòlɔnɛ ŋutɔ. Ale Isak ƒe ake fa le dada ƒe ku ŋuti.

ref: and they had six sons: Zimran, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak, and Shuah.
text: Eya dzi Simran, Yoksan, Medan, Midian, Yisbak kple Suax nɛ.

ref: Later, Jokshan became the father of Sheba and Dedan, and when Dedan grew up, he had three sons: Asshurim, Letushim, and Leummim.
text: Yoksan dzi Seba kple Dedan; eye Asuritɔwo, Letusitɔwo kple Leumitɔwo dzɔ tso Dedan me.

ref: Midian also had five sons: Ephah, Epher, Hanoch, Abida, and Eldaah.
text: Eye Midianviwo nye: Efa, Efer, Henox, Abida kple Edaa. Ame siawo katã dzɔ tso Ketura me.

ref: While Abraham was still alive, he gave gifts to the sons of Hagar and Keturah. He also sent their sons to live in the east far from his son Isaac, and when Abraham died, he left everything to Isaac. The Death of Abraham
text: Ke Abraham na nu srɔ̃ bubuawo ƒe viwo heɖe wo ɖa le Isak ŋu, eye wòɖo wo ɖe ɣedzeƒenyigba dzi le eya ŋutɔ ƒe agbenɔɣi me. Abraham ƒe ku kple eɖiɖi.

ref: Abraham died at the ripe old age of
text: Ke Abraham ku amegã ɖeɖi, eye agbenɔnɔ tii hafi wòku, eye wòyi ɖe tɔgbuiawo gbɔ.

ref: God blessed Isaac after this, and Isaac moved to a place called “The Well of the Living One Who Sees Me.” Ishmael's Descendants
text: Le Abraham ƒe ku megbe la Mawu yra via Isak. Eye Isak nɔ „Agbetɔ, si le kpɔyem la“ ƒe vudo gbɔ. Ismael ƒe dzidzimeviwo.

ref: Ishmael was the son of Abraham and Hagar, the slave woman of Sarah.
text: Esiawo nye Abrahamvi Ismael, amesi Egipte-nyɔnu Hagar, Sara ƒe kosi, dzi na Abraham la ƒe dzidzimeviwo.

ref: Ishmael had twelve sons, in this order: Nebaioth, Kedar, Adbeel, Mibsam,
text: Esiawo nye Ismaelviwo ƒe ŋkɔwo le woƒe ƒomeŋkɔwo nu. Nebayot, Ismael ƒe ŋgɔgbevi, Kedar, Adbeel, Mibsam,

ref: Mishma, Dumah, Massa,
text: Misma, Duma, Masa,

ref: Hadad, Tema, Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah.
text: Xadad, Tema, Yetur, Nafis kple Kedma.

ref: Each of Ishmael's sons was a tribal chief, and a village was named after each of them.
text: Ame siawo nye Ismaelviwo kple woƒe ŋkɔwo. Fia wuieve le woƒe aƒetsotsowo, agbadɔwo kple woƒe dukɔwo nu.

ref: Ishmael had settled in the land east of his brothers, and his sons settled everywhere from Havilah to Shur, east of Egypt on the way to Asshur. Ishmael was
text: Eye woƒe nɔƒe tso Xawila vaseɖe Sur le Egipte ƒe ɣedzeƒe heɖo ta Asur ke; ale wɔ womimi wo nɔviwo katã ɖo. Esau kple Yakob.

ref: Isaac was the son of Abraham,
text: Esia nye Abraham vi Isak ƒe dzidzimeŋutinya: Abraham ye dzi Isak.

ref: Rebekah still had no children. So Isaac asked the Lord to let her have a child, and the Lord answered his prayer.
text: Ke Isak, ƒo koko na Yehowa le srɔ̃a ta, elabena etsi ko. Eye Yehowa ɖo to eƒe kokoƒoƒo la, eye srɔ̃a Rebeka fɔ fu.

ref: Before Rebekah gave birth, she knew she was going to have twins, because she could feel them inside her, fighting each other. She thought, “Why is this happening to me?” Finally, she asked the Lord why her twins were fighting,
text: Ke esi ɖeviawo le ŋu ɖom wo nɔewo le eƒe dɔ me la, egblɔ bena: Ne ele alea la, nukaŋuti megale agbe ɖo? Eye wòyi ɖabia Yehowa se.

ref: and he told her: “Your two sons will become two separate nations. The younger of the two will be stronger, and the older son will be his servant.”
text: Eye Yehowa gblɔ nɛ bena; Dukɔ evee le wò dɔ me, eye dukɔ eveawo amã atso wò dɔ me ke; dukɔ ɖeka asesẽ awu ɖeka, eye tsitsitɔ asubɔ ɖevitɔ!

ref: the first baby was covered with red hair, so he was named Esau.
text: Esi gbãtɔ do la, eŋuti bia hẽ, eye fu le eŋu kedzekedze, eyata woyɔ eƒe ŋkɔ bena Esau.

ref: The second baby grabbed on to his brother's heel, so they named him Jacob. Isaac was
text: Eyome nɔvia hã do, eye wòlé Esau ƒe afɔkpodzi ɖe asi; eyata woyɔ eƒe ŋkɔ bena Yakob. Isak xɔ ƒe blaade, eye wòdzi wo.

ref: As Jacob and Esau grew older, Esau liked the outdoors and became a good hunter, while Jacob lived the quiet life of a shepherd.
text: Esi ɖeviawo va tsi la, Esau zu adela, eye wòlɔ̃a gbe me nɔnɔ; ke Yakob enye ame fafa, eye wòlɔ̃a agbadɔ me nɔnɔ.

ref: Esau would take the meat of wild animals to his father Isaac, so Isaac loved him more, but Jacob was his mother's favorite son.
text: Eye Isak lɔ̃a Esau, elabena adelã vivia enu; ke Rebeka ya lɔ̃a Yakob. Esau dzra eƒe ŋgɔgbevinyenye.

ref: One day, when Jacob was cooking some stew, Esau came home hungry
text: Gbe ɖeka Yakob ɖa nu, eye Esau gbɔ tso gbe me, eye dɔ wui ɖi tsi ɖe eŋu.

ref: and said, “I'm starving to death! Here and now give me some of that red stew!” That's how Esau got the name “Edom.”
text: Tete Esau gblɔna Yakob bena: Na nu dzia ɖem maɖu, elabena ɖɔ wum ɖi tsi ɖe ŋunye. Eyata woyɔ eƒe ŋkɔ be Edom.

ref: Jacob replied, “Sell me your rights as the first-born son.”
text: Yakob ɖo ŋu nɛ be: Tsɔ wò ŋgɔgbevinyenye ƒle nam gbã gbɔ.

ref: “I'm about to die,” Esau answered. “What good will those rights do me?”
text: Tete Esau gblɔ be: Mele kuku ge tso, nuka gɔ̃ ŋgɔgbevinyenye awɔ nam?

ref: But Jacob said, “Promise me your birthrights, here and now!” And that's what Esau did.
text: Eye Yakob gblɔ be: Ka atam ƒu dzi nam gbɔ! Tete wòka atam nɛ, eye wòtsɔ eƒe ŋgɔgbevinyenye ƒle na Yakob.

ref: Jacob then gave Esau some bread and some of the bean stew, and when Esau had finished eating and drinking, he just got up and left, showing how little he thought of his rights as the first-born.
text: Eye Yakob tsɔ abolo kple bɔbɔ na Esau. Tete wòɖu nu no nu, eye wòtso dzo. Ale wo, eye Esau do vlo eƒe ŋgɔgbevinyenye enye esi.

ref: Once during Abraham's lifetime, the fields had not produced enough grain, and now the same thing happened. So Isaac went to King Abimelech of the Philistines in the land of Gerar,
text: Ke do to le anyigba la dzi, abe alesi wòto le Abraham ŋɔli ene; tete Isak ʋu yi ɖe Filistitowo ƒe fia Abimelex gbɔ le Gerar.

ref: because the Lord had appeared to Isaac and said: Isaac, stay away from Egypt! I will show you where I want you to go.
text: Eye Yehowa ɖe eɖokui fiae gblɔ bena: Megayi Egipte o, ke boŋ na anyigba, si mafia wò la dzi!

ref: You will live there as a foreigner, but I will be with you and bless you. I will keep my promise to your father Abraham by giving this land to you and your descendants.
text: Nye amedzro le anyigba sia dzi, eye manɔ anyi kpli wò, eye mayra wò. Elabena matsɔ anyigba blibo la katã ana mia kple wò dzidzimeviwo, eye mawɔ atam, si meka na fofowò Abraham la dzi.

ref: I will give you as many descendants as there are stars in the sky, and I will give your descendants all of this land. They will be a blessing to every nation on earth,
text: Mana wò dzidzimeviwo nasɔ gbɔ abe dziƒo ɣletiviwo ene, eye matsɔ anyigba sia katã ana wò dzidzimeviwo, eye le wò dzidzimeviwo me woayra anyigba dzi dukɔwo katã le.

ref: Isaac moved to Gerar
text: Eye Isak nɔ Gerar.

ref: with his beautiful wife Rebekah. He was afraid that someone might kill him to get her, and so he told everyone that Rebekah was his sister.
text: Ke esi afima ŋutsuwo bia srɔ̃a ta se la, egblɔ bena, ye nɔvinyɔnue. Elabena ele vɔvɔ̃m be, ne yeʋu me be, ye srɔ̃e la, ekema afima ŋutsuwo awu ye le ye srɔ̃ Rebeka ta; elabena edze tugbe ŋutɔ.

ref: After Isaac had been there a long time, King Abimelech looked out a window and saw Isaac hugging and kissing Rebekah.
text: Eye esi wonɔ afima eteƒe didi la, gbe ɖeka Filistitɔwo ƒe fia Abimelex kpɔ, alesi Isak kple srɔ̃a Rebeka wole fefemi, to fesre nu.

ref: Abimelech called him in and said, “Rebekah must be your wife! Why did you say she is your sister?” “Because I thought someone would kill me,” Isaac answered.
text: Tete Abimelex yɔ Isak hegblɔ bena: Kpɔ ɖa, srɔ̃wo wònye tso! Menye wòe gblɔ bena, ye nɔvinyɔnue oa? Isak ɖo ŋu nɛ bena; Elabena mele vɔvɔ̃m be, woawum le eta.

ref: “Don't you know what you've done?” Abimelech exclaimed. “If someone had slept with her, you would have made our whole nation guilty!”
text: Eye Abimelex gblɔ nɛ bena: Nuka enye si nèwɔ ɖe mía ŋu? Esusɔ vie vinyewo dometɔ ɖeka de asi srɔ̃wòa ŋu kloe, eye ahe dzɔgbevɔ̃e gãa ɖe ava mía dzi.

ref: Then Abimelech warned his people that anyone who even touched Isaac or Rebekah would be put to death.
text: Tete Abimelex de se na eƒe dukɔ blibo la katã bena: Amesi ke awɔ nane ɖe ŋutsu sia kple srɔ̃a ŋu la, woawui. Isak kpɔ kesinɔnuwo, eye Filistitɔwo ʋã ŋui.

ref: Isaac planted grain and had a good harvest that same year. The Lord blessed him,
text: Eye Isak ƒã nu ɖe anyigba ma dzi eye wòŋe nu le ƒe ma me wòdzi ɖe dzi zi alafa ɖeka sɔŋ, elabena Yehowa yrae.

ref: and Isaac was so successful that he became very rich.
text: Ale ŋutsu la kpɔ nu gakpɔ nu ɖe dzi, vaseɖe esime woɖo kpo le eme.

ref: In fact, the Philistines were jealous of the large number of sheep, goats, and slaves that Isaac owned,
text: Eye wòkpɔ alẽhawo kple nyihawo hekpe ɖe kluvi geɖewo ŋuti, ale bena Filistitowo ʋã ŋui.

ref: and they stopped up the wells that Abraham's servants had dug before his death.
text: Vudo, siwo katã fofoa ƒe dɔlawo ɖe le fofoa Abraham ŋɔli la, Filistitɔwo yi ɖaxe wo, eye wole anyi ɖe wo me taŋ.

ref: Finally, Abimelech said, “Isaac, I want you to leave our country. You have become too powerful to stay here.”
text: Eye Abimelex gblɔ na Isak bena: Dzo le mía gbɔ, elabena ekpɔ ŋusẽ ɖe mía dzi akpa!

ref: where he cleaned out those wells that the Philistines had stopped up. Isaac also gave each of the wells the same name that Abraham had given to them.
text: Eye Isak na wogaha vudo, siwo woɖe le fofoa Abraham ŋɔli, eye Filistitɔwo va xe wo, le Abraham ƒe ku megbe la me, eye wògatsɔ ŋkɔ na wo, abe alesi fofoa yɔ wòe ene.

ref: While his servants were digging in the valley, they found a spring-fed well.
text: Eye Isak ƒe dɔlawo ɖe vudo ɖe bali la me, eye wotu tsi dzidzi le afima.

ref: But the shepherds of Gerar Valley quarreled with Isaac's shepherds and claimed the water belonged to them. So this well was named “Quarrel,” because they had quarreled with Isaac.
text: Ke Gerartɔwo ƒe lãnyilawo wɔ dzre kple Isak ƒe lãnyilawo gblɔ bena: Mía tɔe nye; tsi la. Eyata wòna ŋkɔ vudo la be Esek, elabena wowɔ dzre ɖe eta.

ref: Isaac's servants dug another well, and the shepherds also quarreled about it. So that well was named “Jealous.”
text: Eyome la wogaɖe vudo bubu, eye wogawɔ dzre ɖe eya hã ta; eyata wòna ŋkɔe bena Sitma.

ref: Finally, they dug one more well. There was no quarreling this time, and the well was named “Lots of Room,” because the Lord had given them room and would make them very successful.
text: Eye wòʋu le afima ɖo ta ŋgɔ, eye wòɖe vudo bubu; ke womewɔ dzre ɖe eya ta o; eyata wòna ŋkɔe be Rehobot hegblɔ be: Azɔ Yehowa na nɔƒe mí, bena míakeke ta ɖe anyigba la dzi.

ref: Isaac went on to Beersheba,
text: Emegbe la eʋu le afima va Beer-Seba.

ref: where the Lord appeared to him that night and told him, “Don't be afraid! I am the God who was worshiped by your father Abraham, my servant. I will be with you and bless you, and because of Abraham I will give you many descendants.”
text: Tete Yehowa ɖe eɖokui fiae le zã ma me gblɔ bena: Nyee nye fofowò Abraham ƒe Mawu! Megavɔ̃ o, elabena nye la meli kpli wò; mayra wò, eye mana wò dzidzimeviwo nasɔ gbɔ ɖe dzi le nye dɔla Abraham ta.

ref: Isaac built an altar there and worshiped the Lord. Then he set up camp, and his servants started digging a well.
text: Tete wòɖi vɔsamlekpui heyɔ Yehowa ƒe ŋkɔ dzi le afima, eye wòtu eƒe agbadɔ ɖe afima. Emegbe Isak fe dɔlawo ɖe vudo ɖe afima hã.

ref: Meanwhile, Abimelech had left Gerar and was taking his advisor Ahuzzath and his army commander Phicol to see Isaac.
text: Gbe ɖeka Abimelex kple xɔlɔ̃a Ahusat kple eƒe aʋafia Pixol wotso Gerar va egbɔ.

ref: When they arrived, Isaac asked, “Why are you here? Didn't you send me away because you hated me?”
text: Eye Isak gblɔ na wo bena: Nuka ta mieva gbɔnye ɖo, esi mielé fum, eye mienyãm le mia gbɔ?

ref: They answered, “We now know for certain that the Lord is with you, and we have decided there needs to be a peace treaty between you and us. So let's make a solemn agreement
text: Wogblɔ nɛ bena: Míekpɔ dze sii keŋ bena, Yehowa li kpli wò; eyata míegblɔ be, anyo be, míaka atam na mía nɔewo, eye míabla nu kpli wò,

ref: not to harm each other. Remember, we have never hurt you, and when we sent you away, we let you go in peace. The Lord has truly blessed you.”
text: be nagawɔ vɔ̃a ɖeke mi o, abe alesi miawo hã miewɔ naneke wò o, ke boŋ nyui sɔŋ ko miewɔ na wò, eye miena nedzo le ŋutifafa me la ene, elabena amesi Yehowa yra nènye.

ref: Isaac gave a big feast for them, and everyone ate and drank.
text: Emegbe eɖo kplɔ̃ na wo, woɖu nu, eye wono nu.

ref: Early the next morning Isaac and the others made a solemn agreement, then he let them go in peace.
text: Esi ŋu ke la, woka atam na wo nɔewo, eye Isak do mɔ wo, eye wodzo le egbɔ le ŋutifafa me.

ref: Later that same day Isaac's servants came and said, “We've struck water!”
text: Gbemagbe ke Isak ƒe dɔlawo va gblɔ nɛ le vudo, si woɖe la ŋuti be: Mieke ɖe tsi ŋu!

ref: So Isaac named the well Shibah, and the town is still called Beersheba. Esau's Foreign Wives
text: Eye wòna ŋkɔe be Siba, eyata du la ƒe ŋkɔ nye Beer-Seba vaseɖe egbegbe. Esau ɖe srɔ̃.

ref: But these two women brought a lot of grief to Esau's parents Isaac and Rebekah.
text: Woawo zu nuxaxa gãa ɖe na Isak kple Rebeka.

ref: Isaac was old and almost blind, when he called in his first-born son Esau, who asked him, “Father, what can I do for you?”
text: Esi Isak ku amegã ɖeɖi, eye eƒe ŋkuwo dzi tsyɔ, ale be megale nu kpɔm o la, eya via xoxoetɔ Esau gblɔ nɛ bena: Vinye! Eɖo ŋu nɛ bena: Nyee nye si.

ref: Isaac replied, “I am old and might die at any time.
text: Tete wògblɔ nɛ bena: Kpɔ ɖa, metsi, eye nyemenya nye kugbe o.

ref: So go hunting with your bow and arrows and kill a wild animal.
text: Eyata tsɔ wò adedanuwo, ɖaku kple da, eye nayi gbe me aɖawu lãa ɖe vɛ nam!

ref: Cook some of that tasty food that I love so much and bring it to me. I want to eat it once more and give you my blessing before I die.”
text: Emegbe naɖa nu nyuia ɖe nam, abe alesi nyoa ŋunye ene, eye natsɔe vɛ nam, ne maɖu, be mayra wò hafi aku!

ref: Rebekah had been listening, and as soon as Esau left to go hunting,
text: Eye Rebeka se nya, siwo Isak gblɔ na via Esau la. Ke esi Esau yi gbe me, be woawu lã atsɔ va aƒee la,

ref: she said to Jacob, “I heard your father tell Esau
text: Rebeka gblɔ na via Yakob be: Mese alesi fofowò gblɔ na noviwò Esau bena:

ref: to kill a wild animal and cook some tasty food for him before he dies. Your father said this because he wants to bless your brother with the Lord as his witness.
text: Di adelã, eye naɖa nu nyuia ɖe vɛ nam, ne maɖu, eye mayra wò le Yehowa ƒe ŋku me hafi aku.

ref: Now, my son, listen carefully to what I want you to do.
text: Eyata vinye, se nye gbe, eye nawɔ nusi magblɔ na wò la!

ref: Go and kill two of your best young goats and bring them to me. I'll cook the tasty food that your father loves so much.
text: Yi lãhawo gbɔ, eye nalé gbɔ̃vi nyui eve vɛ nam, bena maɖa nu nyuia ɖe na fofowò, abe alesi nyoa eŋu ene.

ref: Then you can take it to him, so he can eat it and give you his blessing before he dies.”
text: Emegbe natsɔe ayi na fofowò, ne wòaɖu, eye wòayra wò hafi aku.

ref: “My brother Esau is a hairy man,” Jacob reminded her. “And I am not.
text: Tete Yakob gblɔ na dadaa Rebeka bena: Enyo hafi, gake nɔvinye Esau la ɖu me wònye, ke nye la fu mele ŋunye o.

ref: If my father touches me and realizes I am trying to trick him, he will put a curse on me instead of giving me a blessing.”
text: Misela fofonye ali asi ŋunye akpɔ, ekema abum ameflulae, eye mayram o, ke aƒo fi boŋ adem.

ref: Rebekah insisted, “Let his curse fall on me! Just do what I say and bring me the meat.”
text: Dadaa ɖo ŋu ne bena: Vinye, nye boŋ ŋue wò fiƒoɖe la laɖi; ɖo tom ko, eye nayi aɖalée vɛ!

ref: So Jacob brought the meat to his mother, and she cooked the tasty food that his father liked.
text: Tete wòyi ɖalée vɛ na dadaa, eye dadaa ɖa nu nyuia ɖe, abe alesi nyoa fofoa ŋu ene.

ref: Then she took Esau's best clothes and put them on Jacob.
text: Emegbe la Rebeka tsɔ via xoxoetɔ Esau ƒe atsyɔɖowu, siwo le egbɔ la, hedo na via ɖevitɔ Yakob.

ref: She also covered the smooth part of his hands and neck with goatskins
text: Eye wotso gbɔ̃viawo ƒe agbalẽ bla ɖe eƒe asiwò kple eƒe ko, siwo ŋu ɖi niŋɔniŋɔ la ŋu.

ref: and gave him some bread and the tasty food she had cooked.
text: Eye wòtsɔ nuɖuɖu la kple abolo, si wòƒo la, ɖe asi na via Yakob.

ref: Jacob went to his father and said, “Father, here I am.” “Which one of my sons are you?” his father asked.
text: Tete wòyi ɖe fofoa gbɔ gblɔ bena: Fofonye! Eɖo ŋu bena: Nye enye si! Vinye, wò amekae?

ref: Jacob replied, “I am Esau, your first-born, and I have done what you told me. Please sit up and eat the meat I have brought. Then you can give me your blessing.”
text: Yakob gblɔ na fofoa bena: Nye enye Esau, wò ŋgɔgbevi, mewɔ abe alesi nègblɔ nam ene. Fɔ ɖe ali nu, ne naɖu nye adelã la, eye nayram.

ref: Isaac asked, “My son, how did you find an animal so quickly?” “The Lord your God was kind to me,” Jacob answered.
text: Tete Isak gblɔ na via bena: Aleke wɔ nèkpɔe kaba ale, vinye? Eɖo ŋu nɛ bena: Elabena wò Mawu la na wòva ɖo gom.

ref: “My son,” Isaac said, “come closer, where I can touch you and find out if you really are Esau.”
text: Isak gblɔ na Yakob bena: Te ɖe ŋunye, vinye, ne mali asi ŋuwò akpɔ, be vinye Esau nènye loo, alo menye eyae o!

ref: Jacob went closer. His father touched him and said, “You sound like Jacob, but your hands feel hairy like Esau's.”
text: Tete Yakob te ɖe fofoa Isak ŋu, eye esi wòli asi eŋu kpɔ la, egblɔ bena: Gbe la enye Yakob ƒe gbe, ke asiawo nye Esau ƒe asi.

ref: And so Isaac blessed Jacob, thinking he was Esau.
text: Ale medze sii o, elabena eƒe asiwò to fu kedzekedze abe nɔvia Esau ƒe asi ene, eye wòyrae.

ref: Isaac asked, “Are you really my son Esau?” “Yes, I am,” Jacob answered.
text: Eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Vinye Esau nènyea? Eɖo ŋu bena: E, nyee!

ref: So Isaac told him, “Serve me the wild meat, and I can give you my blessing.” Jacob gave him some meat, and he ate it. He also gave him some wine, and he drank it.
text: Eye wògblɔ bena: Tsɔe vɛ nam, ne maɖu vinye ƒe adelã la, eye mayra wò! Tete wòtsɔe ve nɛ woɖu, emegbe wotsɔ wein vɛ nɛ wònɔ.

ref: Then Isaac said, “Son, come over here and kiss me.”
text: Eyome la fofoa Isak gblɔ nɛ bena: Te ɖe ŋunye azɔ, vinye, eye naɖuɖɔ nu nam!

ref: While Jacob was kissing him, Isaac caught the smell of his clothes and said: “The smell of my son is like a field the Lord has blessed.
text: Esi wote ɖe eŋu ɖuɖɔ nu nɛ la, eʋẽ eƒe awu me se; eye wòyrae gblɔ bena: Ehɛ̃, vinye le ʋeʋẽm abe alesi gbe, si Yehowa yra, ʋẽnae ene.

ref: God will bless you, my son, with dew from heaven and with fertile fields, rich with grain and grapes.
text: Mawu ana dziƒozãmu kple anyigba wɔnu wò, eye àkpɔ bli kple wein geɖe fũ ŋutɔ.

ref: Nations will be your servants and bow down to you. You will rule over your brothers, and they will kneel at your feet. Anyone who curses you will be cursed; anyone who blesses you will be blessed.”
text: Dukɔwo asubɔ wò, eye ƒomewo anɔ wò ŋusẽ te. Aɖu aƒetɔ ɖe nɔviwòwo nu, eye dawòviwo adze klo na wò. Amesi ke aƒo fi ade wò la, woaƒo fi adee, eye amesi ke ayra wò la, wõayrae!

ref: Right after Isaac had given Jacob his blessing and Jacob had gone, Esau came back from hunting.
text: Ke esi Isak wu Yakob yayra nu, eye Yakob ɖo go tso fofoa Isak gbɔ la, tete nɔvia Esau gbɔ tso adegbe.

ref: He cooked the tasty food, brought it to his father, and said, “Father, please sit up and eat the meat I have brought you, so you can give me your blessing.”
text: Eye eya hã ɖa du nyuia ɖe tsɔ vɛ na fofoa, eye wògblɔ na fofoa bena: Fofonye, fɔ ɖe ali nu, eye naɖu viwò ƒe adelã la, bena nayram!

ref: “Who are you?” Isaac asked. “I am Esau, your first-born son.”
text: Fofoa Isak gblɔ nɛ bena: Wò amekae? Eɖo ŋu bena: Nye enye Esau, wò ŋgɔgbevi.

ref: Isaac started trembling and said, “Then who brought me some wild meat right before you came in? I ate it and gave him a blessing that cannot be taken back.”
text: Tete Isak ɖi vo ŋutɔ gblɔ bena: Ameka gɔ̃ enye si tsɔ adelã vɛ nam, eye meɖu nuawo katã ƒe ɖe, hafi neva, eye meyrae mahã? Eya tɔ ko yayra la anye!

ref: Esau cried out in great distress, “Father, give me a blessing too!”
text: Esi Esau se fofoa ƒe nyawo la, edo ɣli sesĩe nublanuitɔe gblɔ na fofoa bena: Fofonye, yra nye hã!

ref: Isaac answered, “Your brother tricked me and stole your blessing.”
text: Ke egblɔ bena: Nɔviwò to aye meva xɔwo yayra la xoxo.

ref: Esau replied, “My brother deserves the name Jacob, because he has already cheated me twice. The first time he cheated me out of my rights as the first-born son, and now he has cheated me out of my blessing.” Then Esau asked his father, “Don't you have any blessing left for me?”
text: Eɖo ŋu bena: Yakob ye woyɔnɛ vavã! Zi eve enye si wòblem: Exɔ ŋgɔgbevinyenye le asinye, azɔ egaxɔ nye yayra hã! Eye wòbiae bena: Mèkpɔtɔ yayraa ɖeke ɖi nam oa?

ref: “My son,” Isaac answered, “I have made Jacob the ruler over you and your brothers, and all of you will be his servants. I have also promised him all the grain and grapes that he needs. There's nothing left that I can do for you.”
text: Tete Isak gblɔ na Esau bena: Kpɔ ɖa, meɖoe aƒetɔ ɖe dziwò, metsɔ nɔviawo katã wɔ eƒe dɔlawoe, eye metsɔ bli kple wein hã nɛ: azɔ nuka gɔ̃e gali mawɔ na wò, vinye?

ref: “Father,” Esau asked, “don't you have more than one blessing? You can surely give me a blessing too!” Then Esau started crying again.
text: Esau xɔ edzi na fofoa bena: Alo yayra sia ɖeka koe le asiwòa, fofonye? Yra nye hã, fofonye! Eye Esau gbã avi doo.

ref: So his father said: “Your home will be far from that fertile land, where dew comes down from the heavens.
text: Tete fofoa Isak gblɔ nɛ bena: Manɔ anyigba wɔnu dzi akpɔ o, eye dziƒozãmua ɖeke madza na wò o.

ref: You will live by the power of your sword and be your brother's slave. But when you decide to be free, you will break loose.”
text: Wò yi anyi wò, eye àsubɔ nɔviwò. Ke ne èle ʋiʋlim la, aɖe eƒe kɔkuti ɖa le wò kɔ dzi. Esau ɖi bena, yeawu nɔvia.

ref: Esau hated his brother Jacob because he had stolen the blessing that was supposed to be his. So he said to himself, “Just as soon as my father dies, I'll kill Jacob.”
text: Ke Esau lé Yakob ɖe fu me le yayra, si fofoa tsɔ nɛ la ta, eye Esau ɖo ta me gblɔ bena: Ne fofonye aku, eye woanɔ fu nyim nɛ la, ekema mawu nɔvinye Yakob!

ref: When Rebekah found out what Esau planned to do, she sent for Jacob and told him, “Son, your brother Esau is just waiting for a chance to kill you.
text: Esi Rebeka se via xoxoetɔ Esau ƒe nyawo la, ena woyɔ via ɖevitɔ Yakob vɛ, eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Kpɔ ɖa, nɔviwò Esau ɖo be, yeabia hlɔ̃ awu wò.

ref: Now listen carefully and do what I say. Go to the home of my brother Laban in Haran
text: Eyata vinye, ɖo to nye gbe! Tso, eye nasi ayi ɖe nɔvinye Laban gbɔ le Xaran!

ref: and stay with him for a while. When Esau stops being angry
text: Eye nanɔ egbɔ ŋkekea ɖewo, vaseɖe esime nɔviwò ƒe dziku la nu nata nɛ,

ref: and forgets what you have done to him, I'll send for you to come home. Why should I lose both of my sons on the same day?”
text: vaseɖe esime dzi nadzudzɔ ekuku ɖe ŋuwò, eye wòaŋlɔ nusi nèwɔ ɖe eŋu la be! Ekema mado woakplɔ wò tso afima vɛ. Nukaŋuti mi ame eve miabu ɖem gbe ɖeka?

ref: Rebekah later told Isaac, “Those Hittite wives of Esau are making my life miserable! If Jacob marries a Hittite woman, I'd be better off dead.”
text: Eye Rebeka gblɔ na Isak bena: Agbenɔnɔ tim le Xitivi-nyɔnuawo ta. Ne Yakob hã aɖe anyigba sia dzi tɔ, siwo nye Xitiviwo ƒe vinyɔnua ɖe la, ekema nukaŋuti megale agbe ɖo?

ref: Isaac called in Jacob, then gave him a blessing, and said: Don't marry any of those Canaanite women.
text: Emegbe la Isak yɔ Yakob vɛ, eye wòyrae, eye wòde se nɛ gblɔ nɛ bena: Megaɖe srɔ̃ tso Kanaan-vinyɔnuwo dome o!

ref: Go at once to your mother's father Bethuel in northern Syria and choose a wife from one of the daughters of Laban, your mother's brother.
text: Yi Mesopotamia ɖe dawo fofo Betuel ƒe me, eye naɖe dawo nɔvi Laban ƒe vinyɔnua ɖe!

ref: I pray that God All-Powerful will bless you with many descendants and let you become a great nation.
text: Mawu ŋusẽkatãtɔ nayra wo, eye wòana nadzi asɔ gbɔ ɖe dzi, bena natrɔ azu dukɔ geɖe.

ref: May he bless you with the land he promised Abraham, so that you will take over this land where we now live as foreigners.
text: Eye woatsɔ Abraham ƒe yayra ana mia kple wò dzidzimeviwo, bena nanyi anyigba, si Mawu tsɔ na Abraham, si dzi nènye amedzro le la dome.

ref: Isaac then sent Jacob to stay with Rebekah's brother Laban, the son of Bethuel the Aramean. Esau Marries the Daughter of Ishmael
text: Tete Isak do mɔ Yakob, eye wòyi ɖe Aramtɔ Betuel vi Laban, amesi enye Yakob kple Esau dada Rebeka nɔvi la gbɔ. Esau gaɖe srɔ̃.

ref: Esau found out that his father Isaac had blessed Jacob and had warned him not to marry any of the Canaanite women. He also learned that Jacob had been sent to find a wife in northern Syria
text: Ke esi Esau kpɔ bena, Isak yra Yakob, eye wòɖoe ɖe Mesopotamia, bena wòaɖe srɔ̃ le afima, eye wòyrae de se nɛ gblɔ bena: Megaɖe srɔ̃ tso Kanaan-vinyɔnuwo dome o,

ref: and that he had obeyed his father and mother.
text: eye bena Yakob ɖo to fofoa kple dadaa, eye wòyi Mesopotamia la,

ref: Esau already had several wives, but he now realized how much his father hated the Canaanite women.
text: Esau dze sii bena, Kanaan-vinyɔnuawo medze fofoa Isak ŋu o.

ref: So he married Ishmael's daughter Mahalath, who was the sister of Nebaioth and the granddaughter of Abraham. Jacob's Dream at Bethel
text: Eyata Esau yi ɖe Ismael gbɔ, eye wòɖe Mahalat, Abraham vi Ismael ƒe vinyɔnu, si enye Nebayot nɔvinyɔnu, hekpe ɖe srɔ̃ bubuawo ŋu. Yakob kpɔ atsrɔe, si tɔ dziƒo.

ref: Jacob left the town of Beersheba and started out for Haran.
text: Ke Yakob dze mɔ tso Beer-Seba heyina Xaran.

ref: At sunset he stopped for the night and went to sleep, resting his head on a large rock.
text: Eye wòva ɖo teƒea ɖe hetsi afima dɔ, ela bena ɣe ɖo to xoxo. Tete wòmia anyi ɖe afima, eye wòtsɔ afima kpea ɖe heziɔ tae.

ref: In a dream he saw a ladder that reached from earth to heaven, and God's angels were going up and down on it.
text: Eye wòku drɔ̃e kpɔ atsrɔea ɖe tɔ anyigba, eye eƒe nugbɔ de dziƒo, eye wòkpɔ mawudɔlawo le eflɔm le dzi yim gale ɖiɖim.

ref: The Lord was standing beside the ladder and said: I am the Lord God who was worshiped by Abraham and Isaac. I will give to you and your family the land on which you are now sleeping.
text: Eye wòkpɔ Yehowa le tsitre ɖe eŋgɔ gblɔ bena: Nye enye Yehowa, fofowò Abraham ƒe Mawu kple Isak ƒe Mawu: Anyigba, si dzi nèmlɔ la, màtsɔe ana mia kple wò dzidzimeviwo.

ref: Your descendants will spread over the earth in all directions and will become as numerous as the specks of dust. Your family will be a blessing to all people.
text: Eye wò dzidzimeviwo asɔ gbɔ abe ʋuʋudedi ene, eye nakeke ta ɖe ɣetoɖoƒe, ɣedzeƒe, anyiehe kple dziehe, eye woayra anyigba dzi dukɔwo katã. Ne mia kple wò dzidzimeviwo me.

ref: Wherever you go, I will watch over you, then later I will bring you back to this land. I won't leave you—I will do all I have promised.
text: Manɔ anyi kpli wò, eye afisiafi, si nayi la, makpɔ dziwò, eye mana nagatrɔ ava anyigba sia dzi. Elabena nyemagblẽ wò ɖi, vaseɖe esime mawɔ nya, siwo megblɔ na wò la dzi o.

ref: Jacob woke up suddenly and thought, “The Lord is in this place, and I didn't even know it.”
text: Esi Yakob nyɔ tso alɔ me la, egblɔ be: Vavã, Yehowa le afisia, gake nyemenyae o!

ref: Then Jacob became frightened and said, “What a frightening place! It must be the house of God and the gateway to heaven.”
text: Eye wòyɔ gblɔ bena: Ŋɔdziƒe ka enye si! Afi sia menye afia ɖeke o, negbe Mawu ƒe aƒe kple dziƒogbo ko wonye. Yakob ƒe adzɔgbeɖeɖe.

ref: and he named the place Bethel. Before that it had been named Luz.
text: Eye wòna ŋkɔ teƒe ma bena: Betel; ke tsã la woyɔnɛ be Lus.

ref: Jacob solemnly promised God, “If you go with me and watch over me as I travel, and if you give me food and clothes
text: Eye Yakob ɖe adzɔgbe gblɔ bena: Ne Mawu anɔ anyi kplim, eye wòakpɔ dzinye le mɔ, si dzi mele la dzi, eye wòana abolom maɖu kple awum mado,

ref: and bring me safely home again, you will be my God.
text: eye wòana maganɔ ava fofonye ƒe me dedie la, ekema Yehowa anye nye Mawu.

ref: This rock will be your house, and I will give back to you a tenth of everything you give me.”
text: Eye kpe sia, si meɖo dzesikpee la, anye Mawu ƒe aƒe, eye matsɔ nusianu, si nanam la ƒe ewolia na wò kokoko.

ref: As Jacob continued on his way to the east,
text: Tete Yakob dze mɔ, eye wòyi ɖe ɣetoɖoƒetɔwo ƒe anyigba dzi

ref: he looked out in a field and saw a well where shepherds took their sheep for water. Three flocks of sheep were lying around the well, which was covered with a large rock.
text: Eye wòkpɔ vudoa de le gbea dzi, eye alẽha etɔ̃ wotsyɔ akɔ anyi ɖe afima; elabena vudo sia me wokua tsi lena na alẽhawo wonona, eye kpe gãa ɖe le vudo la nu.

ref: Shepherds would roll the rock away when all their sheep had gathered there. Then after the sheep had been watered, the shepherds would roll the rock back over the mouth of the well.
text: Eye ne alẽhawo katã va ƒo ƒu ɖe afima la, ekema womlia kpe la le vudoa nu, eye wonaa tsi alẽawo; emegbe wogamlia kpe la tua vudoa nu ɖe eteƒe.

ref: Jacob asked the shepherds, “Where are you from?” “We're from Haran,” they answered.
text: Yakob bia wo bena: Nɔvinyewo, afika mietso? Woɖo ŋu bena. Míetso Xaran!

ref: Then he asked, “Do you know Nahor's grandson Laban?” “Yes we do,” they replied.
text: Egblɔ na wo bena: Mienyã Naxor vi Laban mahã? Woɖo ŋu bena: Ẽ, mienyae!

ref: “How is he?” Jacob asked. “He's fine,” they answered. “And here comes his daughter Rachel with the sheep.”
text: Egabia wo bena: Eli sesĩe mahã? Woɖo ŋu bena: Eli sesĩe! Kpɔ ɖa, via Raxel hã kplɔ alẽwo gbɔnae.

ref: Jacob told them, “Look, the sun is still high up in the sky, and it's too early to bring in the rest of the flocks. Water your sheep and take them back to the pasture.”
text: Eye wògblɔ bena: Kpɔ ɖa, ɣe gale dzi, eye womaƒo alẽwo nu ƒu haɖe o. Mina tsi alẽawo woano, emegbe miana woagaɖu gbe.

ref: But they replied, “We can't do that until they all get here, and the rock has been rolled away from the well.”
text: Wogblɔ bena: Míale ŋui o, vaseɖe esime woaƒo alẽhawo katã nu ƒu; ekema míamli kpe la ɖa le vudoa nu, hafi miana tsi alẽawo woano.

ref: While Jacob was still talking with the men, his cousin Rachel came up with her father's sheep.
text: Esi wògale nu ƒom kpli wo la, tete Raxel kplɔ fofoa ƒe alẽwo vɛ, elabena alẽkplɔla wònye.

ref: He then kissed Rachel and started crying because he was so happy.
text: Emegbe la Yakob ɖuɖɔ nu na Raxel, eye wòɖe asi avifafa me.

ref: He told her that he was the son of her aunt Rebekah, and she ran and told her father about him.
text: Eye Yakob gblɔ na Raxel bena, fofoa nɔvi yenye, eye Rebeka vi yenye; tete wòƒu ɖu yi ɖagblɔe na fofoa.

ref: As soon as Laban heard the news, he ran out to meet Jacob. He hugged and kissed him and brought him to his home, where Jacob told him everything that had happened.
text: Esi Laban se nya siawo le nɔvianyɔnu ƒe vi Yakob ŋuti la, eƒu ɖu yi ɖakpee, eye wòkpla asi kɔ nɛ heɖuɖɔ nu nɛ, eye wòkplɔe va eƒe me. Eye wòxlẽ nya, siwo katã va dzɔ la, na Laban.

ref: Laban said, “You are my nephew, and you are like one of my own family.” Jacob Marries Leah and Rachel After Jacob had been there for a month,
text: Tete Laban gblɔ nɛ bena: Nyateƒee, nɔvinye kpamkpam nènye! Ke esi wònɔ egbɔ ɣleti ɖeka la,

ref: Laban said to him, “You shouldn't have to work without pay, just because you are a relative of mine. What do you want me to give you?”
text: Laban gblɔ na Yakob bena: Nɔvinye nènye! Ɖe nawɔ ɖɔ nam dzodzroa? Gblɔe nam, nuka lanye wò fetu?

ref: Laban had two daughters. Leah was older than Rachel, but her eyes didn't sparkle, while Rachel was beautiful and had a good figure.
text: Lea ƒe ŋkuwo dzi wɔ dɔ; ke Raxel ya nyo, eye wòdze tugbe.

ref: Since Jacob was in love with Rachel, he answered, “If you will let me marry Rachel, I'll work seven years for you.”
text: Eye Yakob lɔ̃a Raxel, eyata wògblɔ bena: Masubɔ wò ƒe adre le Raxel, wò nyɔnuvi ɖevitɔ la ta.

ref: Laban replied, “It's better for me to let you marry Rachel than for someone else to have her. So stay and work for me.”
text: Laban gblɔ bena: Enyo be, màtsɔe na wò, wu esi màtsɔe na ŋutsu bubu; nɔ gbɔnye!

ref: Jacob worked seven years for Laban, but the time seemed like only a few days, because he loved Rachel so much.
text: Ale Yakob subɔ ƒe adre ɖe Raxel ta, eye wòle nɛ abe ŋkekea ɖewo ko ene, le alesi gbegbe wòlɔ̃ne la ta. Yakob ɖe srɔ̃.

ref: Jacob said to Laban, “The time is up, and I want to marry Rachel now!”
text: Emegbe la Yakob gblɔ na Laban bena: Tsɔ srɔ̃nye nam azɔ, elabena nye ŋkekewo ɖe, bena maɖee!

ref: So Laban gave a big feast and invited all their neighbors.
text: Tete Laban kpe dua me ŋutsuwo katã, eye wòɖo kplɔ̃ na wo.

ref: But that evening he brought Leah to Jacob, who married her and spent the night with her.
text: Ke esi zã ɖo la, ekplɔ vianyɔnu Lea yi nɛ, eye wòdɔ kplii.

ref: Laban also gave Zilpah to Leah as her servant woman.
text: Eye Laban tsɔ eƒe kosi Silpa na via Lea, bena woasubɔe.

ref: The next morning Jacob found out that he had married Leah, and he asked Laban, “Why did you do this to me? Didn't I work to get Rachel? Why did you trick me?”
text: Ke esi ŋu ke la, ekpɔ be, Lea ye. Eye wògblɔ na Laban bena: Nuka enye si nèwɔ ɖe ŋunye? Alo menye Raxel ta mesubɔ wò ɖo oa? Nukaŋuti nèblema?

ref: Laban replied, “In our country the older daughter must get married first.
text: Laban ɖo ŋu ne bena: Menye míaƒe kɔ be, woatre ɖevitɔ ɖeɖe na xoxoetɔ o.

ref: After you spend this week with Leah, you may also marry Rachel. But you will have to work for me another seven years.”
text: Ne ewu kwasiɖa sia nu la, ekema matsɔ eya hã na wò ɖe ɖɔ, si nagawɔ nam le ƒe adre bubu me la nu.

ref: The Lord knew that Jacob loved Rachel more than he did Leah, and so he gave children to Leah, but not to Rachel.
text: Ke esi Yehowa kpɔ bena, womelɔ̃a Lea ƒe nya o la, enu eƒe vidzidɔ, eye wòxe Raxel tɔ.

ref: Leah gave birth to a son and named him Reuben. Then she said, “The Lord has taken away my sorrow. Now my husband will love me more than he does Rachel.”
text: Eye Lea fɔ fu dzi ŋutsuvi, eye wòna ŋkɔe bena Ruben; elabena egblɔ be: Yehowa kpɔ nye nublanui, azɔ srɔ̃nye alɔ̃m.

ref: She had a second son and named him Simeon, because she said, “The Lord has heard that my husband doesn't love me.”
text: Eyome la egafɔ fu, eye wòdzi ŋutsuvi. Eye wògblɔ bena: Yehowa se bena, womelɔ̃a nye nya o; eyata wotso esia hã nam. Eye wòna ŋkɔe bena Simeon.

ref: When Leah's third son was born, she said, “Now my husband will hold me close.” So this son was named Levi.
text: Emegbe wògafɔ fu dzi ŋutsuvi, eye wògblɔ bena: Azɔ ko srɔ̃nye aku ɖe ŋunye, elabena medzi ŋutsuvi etɔ nɛ. Eyata wòna ŋkɔe be Lewi.

ref: She had one more son and named him Judah, because she said, “I'll praise the Lord!”
text: Eye wògafɔ fu dzi ŋutsuvi, eye wògblɔ bena: Azɔ la makafu Yehowa! Eyata wòna ŋkɔe bena Yuda. Emegbe la edzudzɔ vidzidzi.

ref: Rachel was very jealous of Leah for having children, and she said to Jacob, “I'll die if you don't give me some children!”
text: Ke esi Raxel kpɔ bena, yemedzi via ɖeke na Yakob o la, Raxel ʋã ŋu nɔvianyɔnu la, eye wògblɔ na Yakob bena: Na madzi vi; ne menye nenem o la, maku!

ref: But Jacob became upset with Rachel and answered, “Don't blame me! I'm not God.”
text: Tete Yakob do dɔmedzoe ɖe Raxel ŋuti gblɔ bena: Alo Mawu ye menye, amesi xe wò vidzidɔa?

ref: “Here, take my servant Bilhah,” Rachel told him. “Have children by her, and I'll let them be born on my knees to show that they are mine.”
text: Eye wògblɔ bena: Nye kosi Bilha enye si, ɖɔ kplii, bena wòadzi vi ɖe nye akɔ, eye maxɔ mamayɔvi tso egbɔ!

ref: Then Rachel let Jacob marry Bilhah,
text: Eye wòtsɔ eƒe kosi Bilha ne wòde, eye Yakob dɔ kplii.

ref: and they had a son.
text: Eye Bilha fɔ fu dzi ŋutsuvi na Yakob.

ref: Rachel named him Dan, because she said, “God has answered my prayers. He judged in my favor and has given me a son.”
text: Tete Raxel gblɔ bena: Mawu tso afia nam, ese nye gbe, eye wòna ŋutsuvim. Eyata wòna ŋkɔe be Dan.

ref: When Bilhah and Jacob had a second son,
text: Eyome la Raxel ƒe kosi Bilha gafɔ fu, eye wòdzi ŋutsuvi evelia na Yakob.

ref: Rachel said, “I've struggled hard with my sister, and I've won!” So she named the boy Naphtali.
text: Eye Raxel gblɔ bena: Meʋli heƒo koko na Mawu le nɔvinye ta, meɖu dzi Eyata wòna ŋkɔe bena Naftali.

ref: When Leah realized she could not have any more children, she let Jacob marry her servant Zilpah,
text: Ke esi Lea kpɔ bena, yemegale vi dzim o la, etsɔ eƒe kosi Silpa na Yakob wòɖe.

ref: and they had a son.
text: Eye Silpa, Lea ƒe kosi la, dzi ŋutsuvi na Yakob.

ref: “I'm really lucky,” Leah said, and she named the boy Gad.
text: Tete Lea gblɔ bena: Nye Mawu ɖi! Eye wòna ŋkɔe be Gad.

ref: Leah exclaimed, “I'm happy now, and all the women will say how happy I am.” So she named him Asher. Love Flowers
text: Tete Lea gblɔ bena: Mekpɔ dzɔgbe nyui! Azɔ vinyɔnuwo akafum bena, dzɔgbenyuitɔ menye. Eyata wòna ŋkɔe be Aser.

ref: During the time of the wheat harvest, Reuben found some love flowers and took them to his mother Leah. Rachel asked Leah for some of them,
text: Le luŋeɣi la Ruben yi agble me, eye wòkpɔ agbitsa tsɔ vɛ na dada Lea. Tete Raxel gblɔ na Lea bena: Meɖe kuku, na viwò ƒe agbitsa la ɖem!

ref: but Leah said, “It's bad enough that you stole my husband! Now you want my son's love flowers too.” “All right,” Rachel answered. “Let me have the flowers, and you can sleep with Jacob tonight.”
text: Eɖo ŋu nɛ bena: Alo mesɔ gbɔ be, nèxɔ srɔ̃nye le asinye oa? Azɔ nèle didim be, nagaxɔ vinye ƒe agbitsa hãa? Tete Raxel gblɔ bena: Ekema nedɔ gbɔwò egbe zã ɖe viwò ƒe agbitsa la nu!

ref: That evening when Jacob came in from the fields, Leah told him, “You're sleeping with me tonight. I hired you with my son's love flowers.” They slept together that night,
text: Esi Yakob gbɔ tso gbe me le fiẽ la, Lea yi ɖakpee gblɔ bena: Gbɔnyee nava, elabena mexe fe ɖe tawo kple vinye ƒe agbitsa! Eye wòdɔ egbɔ le zã ma me.

ref: and God answered Leah's prayers by giving her a fifth son.
text: Eye Mawu ɖo to Lea, eye wòfɔ fu dzi ŋutsuvi atɔ̃lia na Yakob.

ref: Leah shouted, “God has rewarded me for letting Jacob marry my servant,” and she named the boy Issachar.
text: Eye Lea gblɔ bena: Mawu xe fe nam, elabena metsɔ nye kosi na srɔ̃nye wòɖe. Eyata wòna ŋkɔe be Isaxar.

ref: When Leah had another son,
text: Eyome Lea gafɔ fu, eye wòdzi ŋutsuvi adelia na Yakob.

ref: she exclaimed, “God has given me a wonderful gift, and my husband will praise me for giving him six sons.” So she named the boy Zebulun.
text: Tete Lea gblɔ bena Mawu na nu nyuia ɖem, azɔ srɔ̃nye abum, elabena medzi ŋutsuvi ade nɛ. Eyata wòna ŋkɔe be Zebulon.

ref: Later, Leah had a daughter and named her Dinah.
text: Emegbe la edzi nyɔnuvi, eye wòna ŋkɔe be Dina.

ref: Finally, God remembered Rachel—he answered her prayer by giving her a son. “God has taken away my disgrace,” she said.
text: Eye wòfɔ fu dzi ŋutsuvi, tete wògblɔ bena: Mawu ɖe nye ŋukpe ɖa!

ref: “I'll name the boy Joseph, and I'll pray that the Lord will give me another son.” Jacob and Laban
text: Eyata wòna ŋkɔe be Yosef; elabena egblɔ be: Yehowa agatsɔ ŋutsuvi bubu akpee nam. Yakob ƒe kesinɔnuwo.

ref: After Joseph was born, Jacob said to Laban, “Release me from our agreement and let me return to my own country.
text: Ke esi Raxel dzi Yosef megbe la, Yakob gblɔ na Laban bena: Do mɔm, ne mayi mía de.

ref: You know how hard I've worked for you, so let me take my wives and children and leave.”
text: Tsɔ srɔ̃nye kple vinye, siwo ta mesubɔ wò ɖo la nam, ne madzo; elabena wò ŋutɔ ènya alesi mesubɔ wòe!

ref: But Laban told him, “If you really are my friend, stay on, and I'll pay whatever you ask. I'm sure the Lord has blessed me because of you.”
text: Eye wògayi edzi bena: Gblɔ fetu, si dim nèle la nam, ne mana wò!

ref: Jacob answered: You've seen how hard I've worked for you, and you know how your flocks and herds have grown under my care.
text: Eɖo ŋu nɛ bena: Wò ŋutɔ ènya alesi mesubɔ wòe, kple alesi wò lãha va zu le gbɔnye.

ref: You didn't have much before I came, but the Lord has blessed everything I have ever done for you. Now it's time for me to start looking out for my own family.
text: Nusi le asiwò hafi meva la, ele sue ko; ke azɔ la edzi ɖe dzi ŋutɔ, eye afisiafi, si meto la, Yehowa yraa wò; ke ɣekaɣi gɔ̃e nye hã mawɔ dɔ na ɖokuinye?

ref: “How much do you want me to pay you?” Laban asked. Then Jacob told him: I don't want you to pay me anything. Just do one thing, and I'll take care of your sheep and goats.
text: Tete wògblɔ bena: Nuka mana wò? Yakob ɖo ŋu bena: Nyemaxɔ agaga femee hã le asiwò o, negbe ne alɔ nusi magblɔ na wò la dzi ko; na maganyi wò alẽwo, eye makpɔ wo dzi.

ref: Let me go through your flocks and herds and take the sheep and goats that are either spotted or speckled and the black lambs. That's all you need to give me.
text: Egbe la matsa le wò lãha la katã me, eye maɖe lã, siwo le ŋɔtaŋɔta, kple esiwo tso kpɔ la, ɖe vovo: Alẽvi, siwo le yibɔ, kple gbɔ̃, siwo tso kpɔ, kple esiwo le ŋɔtaŋɔta la, nanye nye fetu!

ref: In the future you can easily find out if I've been honest. Just look and see if my animals are either spotted or speckled, or if the lambs are black. If they aren't, they've been stolen from you.
text: Gbesigbe nava kpɔ nye fetua ɖa la, ekema nye anukwareɖiɖi adze ƒã; ne àkpɔ gbɔ̃, si mele ŋɔtaŋɔta o, kple esi metso kpɔ o, kpakple alẽvi, si mele yibɔ o la, ekema ɖe mefii.

ref: “I agree to that,” was Laban's response.
text: Laban ɖo ŋu bena: Nenɔ nenema, abe alesi negblɔ ene.

ref: Before the end of the day, Laban had separated his spotted and speckled animals and the black lambs from the others and had put his sons in charge of them.
text: Gbemagbe ke wòɖe gbɔ̃tsu, siwo me fli to, kple esiwo tso kpɔ, kple gbɔ̃nɔ, siwo katã le ŋɔtaŋɔta, kple esiwo tso kpɔ — lã sia lã, si me ɣi to — kpakple alẽvi, siwo le yibɔ la, ɖe vovo, eye wòde wo asi na viawo.

ref: Then Laban made Jacob keep the rest of the sheep and goats at a distance of three days' journey.
text: Eye wòɖo be, ye kple Yakob dome mɔ nadidi ŋkeke etɔ, eye Yakob nyi Laban ƒe lã mamleawo.

ref: Jacob cut branches from some poplar trees and from some almond and evergreen trees. He peeled off part of the bark and made the branches look spotted and speckled.
text: Tete Yakob tsɔ logo, xe kple nyati mumuwo, eye woŋlɔ wo ŋu ale: eɖe tsroa ɖe le wo ŋu, eye wòkpɔtɔ ɖe ɖe wo ŋu.

ref: Then he put the branches where the sheep and goats would see them while they were drinking from the water trough. The goats mated there
text: Emegbe la etsɔ ati, siwo woŋlɔ la, da ɖe lãwo ƒe tsinonu me, eye ne lãwo le asi yɔm le tsinɔƒea la, etsɔa wo dana ɖe woƒe ŋku me.

ref: Some of the sheep that Jacob was keeping for Laban were already spotted. And when the others were ready to mate, he made sure that they faced in the direction of the spotted and black ones. In this way, Jacob built up a flock of sheep for himself and did not put them with the other sheep.
text: Eye Yakob ɖe lãviawo ɖo Laban ƒe lã, siwo le ŋɔtaŋɔta, kple esiwo tso kpɔ la kasa. Ale wòɖo lãha tɔxe na eɖokui, eye mena wotɔtɔ kple Laban towo o.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Eye ne lã sesẽtɔwo le asi yɔm la, Yakob tsoa ati ŋɔŋlɔawo dana ɖe lãwo ƒe ŋku me le tsinonu la mè, bena woafɔ fu ɖe ati ŋɔŋlɔawo ŋu.

ref: But he would not put out the branches when the weaker animals were mating. So Jacob got all of the healthy animals, and Laban got what was left.
text: Ke nenye lã beliwoe la, metsɔnɛ ɖoa woƒe ŋku me o; eyata lã beliawo zua Laban tɔ, ke sesẽawo zua Yakob tɔ.

ref: Jacob soon became rich and successful. He owned many sheep, goats, camels, and donkeys, as well as a lot of slaves.
text: Eye ŋutsu la ƒe kesinɔnuwo le dzidzim ɖe dzi ŋutɔ, eye wòkpɔ alẽwo kple gbɔwò, kosiviwo kple kluviwo, kposɔwo kple tedzi geɖewo?

ref: Jacob heard that Laban's sons were complaining, “Jacob is now a rich man, and he got everything he owns from our father.”
text: Ke esi Laban viŋutsuwo gblɔ bena: Yakob da dzo mía fofo ƒe nuwo katã, eye nusiwo nye mía fofo tɔ la, woawoe wòxɔ wɔ eƒe kesinɔnu siawo katãe.

ref: Jacob also noticed that Laban was not as friendly as he had been before.
text: Eye Yakob kpɔ be, Laban megakɔa ŋku me ɖee abe tsã ene o.

ref: One day the Lord said, “Jacob, go back to your relatives in the land of your ancestors, and I will be with you.”
text: Eye Yehowa gblɔ na Yakob bena: Trɔ yi fofowò ƒe anyigba dzi kple ƒowòmeawo gbɔ, eye manɔ anyi kpli wò!

ref: Jacob sent for Rachel and Leah to meet him in the pasture where he kept his sheep,
text: Tete Yakob dɔ woyi ɖayɔ Raxel kple Lea va eƒe lãha gbɔ le gbe me.

ref: and he told them: Your father isn't as friendly with me as he used to be, but the God my ancestors worshiped has been on my side.
text: Eye wògblɔ na wo bena: Mekpɔ be, mia fofo megakɔa ŋku me ɖem abe tsã ene o; gake fofonye ƒe Mawu la li kplim.

ref: years to buy your sheep and goats. During that time you kept changing my wages.
text: Mia ŋutɔwo mienyae bena, nye ŋusẽ katã metsɔ subɔ mia fofo lae.

ref: and that he keeps cheating me by changing my wages time after time. But God has protected me.
text: Gake mia fofo blem, eye wòɖɔ li nye fetu zi ewo; ke Mawu meɖe mɔ nɛ be, wòawɔ vɔ ɖe ŋunye o.
